speaker: Unknown
en: Survival of humanity depends on the successful AI.
zh: 人类的生存取决于成功的 AI。

speaker: Unknown
en: Birth rates are going down.
zh: 出生率正在下降。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you have 60% of your population where you don't have enough people who take care of them, that could cause a lot of human suffering.
zh: 如果你 60% 的人口缺乏足够的人去照顾他们，那可能会造成很多人类的痛苦。

speaker: Unknown
en: When I got this new job, there's zero chance I would have been able to do it if AI wasn't there, because I didn't know anything about so many domains that we were in.
zh: 当我得到这份新工作时，如果没有 AI，我根本没有机会胜任，因为我对我们涉足的这么多领域一无所知。

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of companies are trying to adjust to this new world.
zh: 许多公司正试图适应这个新世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to know the difference between a megatrend and a hype cycle.
zh: 你必须知道 mega trend 和 hype cycle 之间的区别。

speaker: Unknown
en: When there's a megatrend, don't fight it.
zh: 当 mega trend 出现时，不要对抗它。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI is a megatrend, one of the most foundational movements that we have seen in human history.
zh: AI 是一个 mega trend，它是我们在人类历史中见过的最基础的运动之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: To turn Cisco from an older, slower, more traditional enterprise to a very AI-forward company, this is very difficult to do.
zh: 要把 Cisco 从一个老牌、缓慢、更传统的企业变成一个非常 AI-forward 的公司，这是非常难做到的。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI is moving so fast.
zh: AI 发展得太快了。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the things I tell my team is, "Fast-forward six months from now, get prepared for that world."
zh: 我告诉团队的一件事是，“从现在起快进六个月，为那个世界做好准备。”

speaker: Unknown
en: You manage 30,000 people.
zh: 你管理着 30,000 人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every management book that you read will tell you in public criticize in private.
zh: 你读到的每一本管理书籍都会告诉你，要公开批评，私下批评。

speaker: Unknown
en: I fundamentally disagree with that notion.
zh: 我根本不同意这个观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: What you have to do is, is establish enough trust among the team so that you are comfortable critiquing and debating in public.
zh: 你必须做的是，在团队中建立足够的信任，这样你才能在公开场合自在地进行批评和辩论。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's something that you wish you'd known before taking on this role?
zh: 有什么是你在担任这个角色之前希望早知道的？

speaker: Unknown
en: Stamina trumps intellect.
zh: Stamina 胜过 intellect。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's very important to have smart people, but you can become smart if you have curiosity and hunger and staying power and persistence.
zh: 拥有聪明的人非常重要，但如果你有好奇心、渴望心、持久力和毅力，你就能变得聪明。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can't teach hunger.
zh: 渴望心是教不会的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Today, my guest is Jeetu Patel, chief product officer and president at Cisco.
zh: 今天，我的嘉宾是 Jeetu Patel，Cisco 的首席产品官兼总裁。

speaker: Unknown
en: Cisco is not a brand that mostly people think about when they think about AI, but not only are they a massive part of the AI infrastructure build-out that is happening right now all over the world.
zh: Cisco 不是人们在想到 AI 时主要会想到的品牌，但他们不仅是目前正在全球范围内进行的 AI 基础设施建设的重要组成部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: What Jeetu has achieved internally at Cisco in terms of transforming their culture and ways of working to be AI-first is something that most big company leaders only dream about.
zh: Jeetu 在 Cisco 内部在将他们的文化和工作方式转变为 AI-first 方面所取得的成就，是大多数大公司领导者梦寐以求的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Jeetu is also an incredible human with so much warmth and wisdom to share.
zh: Jeetu 也是一个令人难以置信的人，有太多的温暖和智慧可以分享。

speaker: Unknown
en: I am very excited to be sharing his story.
zh: 我很兴奋能分享他的故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't forget to check out lennysproductpass.com for an incredible set of deals available exclusively to Lenny's Newsletter subscribers.
zh: 别忘了去 lennysproductpass.com 看看，那里有专门提供给 Lenny's Newsletter 订阅者的令人难以置信的优惠套餐。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's get into it after a short word from our wonderful sponsors.
zh: 让我们在来自我们优秀赞助商的简短广告之后进入正题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Applications break in all kinds of ways.
zh: 应用程序会以各种方式崩溃。

speaker: Unknown
en: Crashes, slowdowns, regressions, and the stuff that you only see once real users show up.
zh: 崩溃、变慢、回退，以及只有当真实用户出现时你才能看到的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Sentry catches it all.
zh: Sentry 能捕捉这一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: See what happened, where and why, down to the commit that introduced the error, the developer who shipped it, and the exact line of code, all in one connected view.
zh: 看看发生了什么，在哪里以及为什么，一直追踪到引入错误的 commit、发布它的 developer，以及确切的代码行，所有这些都在一个连接的视图中。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've definitely tried the five tabs and Slack thread approach to debugging.
zh: 我肯定试过用五个标签页和 Slack 线程来调试的方法。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is better.
zh: 这个更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Sentry shows you how the request moved, what ran, what slowed down, and what users saw.
zh: Sentry 向你展示请求是如何移动的，运行了什么，什么变慢了，以及用户看到了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: Seer, Sentry's AI debugging agent takes it from there.
zh: Seer，Sentry 的 AI debugging agent 会从那里接手。

speaker: Unknown
en: It uses all of that Sentry context to tell you the root cause, to adjust a fix, and even opens a PR for you.
zh: 它利用所有的 Sentry 上下文来告诉你根本原因，调整修复，甚至为你打开一个 PR。

speaker: Unknown
en: It also reviews your PRs and flags any breaking changes with fixes ready to go.
zh: 它还会审查你的 PR 并标记任何带有修复的破坏性变更。

speaker: Unknown
en: Try Sentry and Seer for free at sentry.io/lenny, and use code Lenny for $100 in Sentry credits.
zh: 在 sentry.io/lenny 免费试用 Sentry 和 Seer，并使用 code Lenny 获得 100 美元的 Sentry credits。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's S-E-N-T-R-Y.io/Lenny.
zh: 那就是 S-E-N-T-R-Y.io/Lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: Your marketing website sets the tone for your brand.
zh: 你的营销网站奠定了你品牌的基调。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is the one touchpoint that every single one of your customers sees.
zh: 这是每一个客户都会看到的触点。

speaker: Unknown
en: In today's age, if you're still having a hard time making small changes and simple updates to it, you are doing something wrong.
zh: 在当今时代，如果你仍然很难对它进行微小的更改和简单的更新，那你肯定是做错了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is why so many companies from early-stage startups to Fortune 500s, including companies like DoorDash, Zapier, Perplexity, and ElevenLabs turn to Framer, the website builder that turns your .com from a formality into a tool for growth.
zh: 这就是为什么从早期初创公司到财富 500 强的这么多公司，包括像 DoorDash、Zapier、Perplexity 和 ElevenLabs 这样的公司，都转向 Framer，这个将你的 .com 从一种形式转变为增长工具的网站构建器。

speaker: Unknown
en: Framer works like your team's favorite design tool and comes with real-time collaboration, a robust CMS with everything you need for great SEO, and advanced analytics that includes integrated A/B testing.
zh: Framer 就像你团队最喜欢的 design tool 一样工作，并带有 real-time collaboration、功能强大的 CMS，拥有你所需要的一切来优化 SEO，以及包括集成 A/B testing 的 advanced analytics。

speaker: Unknown
en: Changes to your Framer site go live to the web in seconds with a single click and without any help from engineering.
zh: 对你 Framer 网站的更改只需点击一下即可在几秒钟内上线 web，无需任何工程帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whether you want to launch a new site, test a few landing pages, or migrate your full .com, Framer has programs for startups, scale-ups and large enterprises to make going from idea to live site as easy and fast as possible.
zh: 无论是你想发布一个新网站、测试几个落地页，还是迁移你的整个 .com，Framer 都有针对初创公司、成长型公司和大型企业的项目，使从想法到上线网站变得尽可能简单和快速。

speaker: Unknown
en: Learn how to turn your website into a growth engine from a Framer expert, or just get started building for free today at framer.com/lenny.
zh: 从 Framer 专家那里学习如何将你的网站变成 growth engine，或者今天就在 framer.com/lenny 免费开始构建。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you're a Lenny's Product Pass subscriber, you get an entire year of Framer Pro for free.
zh: 如果你是 Lenny's Product Pass 的订阅者，你将免费获得一整年的 Framer Pro。

speaker: Unknown
en: Check it out at framer.com/lenny.
zh: 请在 framer.com/lenny 查看详情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Rules and restrictions may apply.
zh: Rules 和 restrictions 可能适用。

speaker: Unknown
en: Jeetu, thank you so much for being here, and welcome to the podcast.
zh: Jeetu，非常感谢你的到来，欢迎来到播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: Lenny, I'm excited.
zh: Lenny，我很兴奋。

speaker: Unknown
en: Good to see you.
zh: 很高兴见到你。

speaker: Unknown
en: The timing of this conversation is so amazing.
zh: 这次对话的时机真是太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're just coming off running the most insane assembling of AI thought leaders and tech leaders I've ever seen.
zh: 你刚刚组织完我见过的最疯狂的 AI 思想领袖和技术领袖的聚会。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let me just read a few of the names that you guys had at the summit that just happened a couple of days ago.
zh: 我就读几个你们几天前的峰会上的名字。

speaker: Unknown
en: You had Jensen, you had Sam Altman, and you had Marc Andreessen, you had Fei-Fei Li, you had the CEO of Intel, AWS, Mike Krieger, Kevin Weil.
zh: 你请到了 Jensen，你请到了 Sam Altman，你请到了 Marc Andreessen，你请到了 Fei-Fei Li，你请到了 Intel 的 CEO，AWS，Mike Krieger，Kevin Weil。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's just like a third of the guests you guys had.
zh: 这大概只占你们嘉宾的三分之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know how you did this, but it feels like you have this fire hose of information coming at you.
zh: 我不知道你是怎么做到的，但这感觉就像你有一股信息流向你喷涌而来。

speaker: Unknown
en: You interviewed a lot of these people on stage, and so while it's fresh in your mind, I want to ask you, after doing this summit, after hearing from these folks, what's something that you've changed your mind about, or what's just like an insight that has been lodged in your head ever since doing the summit?
zh: 你在台上采访了这些人，既然它在你脑海里还很新鲜，我想问你，在举办了这个峰会，听了这些人的发言之后，有什么事情是你改变了想法的，或者有什么见解自从举办峰会以来就一直留在你脑海里？

speaker: Unknown
en: It was an amazing thing to pull off, because we never thought we'd be able to do it, and we were really worried going into it, thinking, "Well, we're trying to do fireside chats for 12 hours, and there's a capacity of human absorption that we're trying to challenge."
zh: 能做成这件事真是太神奇了，因为我们从未想过我们能做到，而且我们在开始之前真的很担心，心想，“好吧，我们要尝试进行 12 小时的炉边谈话，而我们要挑战的是人类的吸收能力。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, we tried to put a lot of breaks in there and we started at nine a.m and we ended at nine p.m., and we had a couple hour break in the middle, but everyone stayed and everyone was engaged and we could have gone until 11 and it would've been fine.
zh: 所以，我们在中间安排了很多休息，我们在早上九点开始，晚上九点结束，中间有几个小时的休息，但大家都留了下来，大家都参与其中，我们可以一直进行到晚上 11 点，那也会没问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's because the quality of the conversations and the caliber of the guests that were there made a world of a difference.
zh: 这是因为对话的质量和到场嘉宾的档次起到了天壤之别的作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: What was the takeaway from it?
zh: 这有什么收获吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'd say a few things.
zh: 我会说有几件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is the capabilities overhang is real.
zh: 第一是能力过剩是真实的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there's more functionality, on one end there's kind of this paradox of progress.
zh: 我认为有更多的功能，一方面有一种进步的悖论。

speaker: Unknown
en: On one end we are solving all these amazing problems with science, on the other end you talk to the enterprise, they're like, "We're struggling with adoption."
zh: 一方面我们正在用科学解决所有这些惊人的问题，另一方面你与企业交谈，他们会说，“我们在采用方面很挣扎。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I feel like there's help that's going to be needed within organizations.
zh: 我觉得组织内部将需要帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the reason we pulled this thing together, the goal was, what is happening in the industry and how can we help customers make sure that they can make the most of it?
zh: 我们把这件事聚在一起的原因是，目标是，行业中正在发生什么，我们如何帮助客户确保他们能充分利用它？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because we are in one of the most foundational movements that we have seen in human history, and it's we got to make sure that we make the most of it.
zh: 因为我们正处于人类历史上所见过的最基础的运动之一，我们必须确保我们要充分利用它。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, that was one is, the capabilities overhang is real.
zh: 所以，这就是第一点，能力过剩是真实的。

speaker: Unknown
en: The second area is, I'd say that it's harder when you go beyond some of the more obvious use cases.
zh: 第二个领域是，我想说，当你超越一些更明显的用例时，事情会更难。

speaker: Unknown
en: For example, coding is a very, very good use case that you're starting to get a lot of success in.
zh: 例如，编码就是一个非常好的用例，你已经开始在这方面获得很多成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, we just had our first product that we think we'll be in the next two weeks, 100% written with AI.
zh: 我的意思是，我们刚刚有了我们的第一个产品，我们想我们会在接下来的两周内，100% 用 AI 编写。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think that's as easy when you go into every other function of the business.
zh: 我认为当你进入业务的其他功能时，这并不那么容易。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that was actually very apparent that hey, this is going to require some nuance and understanding of how every business works.
zh: 这一点实际上非常明显，嘿，这将需要一些细微差别的理解和对每家企业如何运作的理解。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the third one, which is a really interesting takeaway, and Marc Andreessen talked about this in your podcast a few days ago.
zh: 然后是第三点，这是一个非常有趣的收获，Marc Andreessen 几天前在你的播客中谈到了这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, when I talked to him, I actually started with your podcast, because it was so interesting, and then we dug into it a little bit more.
zh: 事实上，当我与他交谈时，我实际上是从你的播客开始的，因为它太有趣了，然后我们更深入地挖掘了它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then Kevin Scott was also talking about this, but this notion of the fact that birth rates are going down and we have a demographic shift that's happening in the world and there's going to be more people that are in the older age bracket than the younger age bracket, and those older people are going to need folks to take care of them.
zh: Kevin Scott 也在谈论这一点，但这个概念是，出生率正在下降，世界上正在发生人口结构的转变，处于老年年龄段的人将比年轻年龄段的人多，而这些老年人将需要有人来照顾他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: And historically in society, that's actually always been the case, but we might be at a point where that might not be the case.
zh: 历史上在社会中，实际上一直如此，但我们可能正处于一个可能并非如此的时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when that's not the case, we worry about AI taking our jobs, I think that survival of humanity depends on a successful AI.
zh: 当情况不再如此时，我们担心 AI 夺走我们的工作，我认为人类的生存取决于成功的 AI。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because at some point if you have 60% of your population that's in a demographic where you don't have enough people who take care of them, that could cause a lot of human suffering.
zh: 因为在某个时刻，如果你有 60% 的人口处于一个人口结构，没有足够的人照顾他们，这可能会导致很多人类的痛苦。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I don't think people talk about this enough, and that's something that we have to take a moment and digest, that this is so important for our collective success moving forward.
zh: 所以，我认为人们对此谈论得不够，我们必须花点时间来消化这一点，这对我们未来的集体成功是如此重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: Something I was going to say during my chat with Marc and when he talked about that AI is basically coming just in time to save us, because there aren't going to be enough people to do the jobs.
zh: 我本来想在与 Marc 的聊天时说这件事，当他谈到 AI 基本上恰逢其时地出现来拯救我们时，因为没有足够的人去做这些工作了。

speaker: Unknown
en: In my head I was thinking, "This is like another signal that we are in a simulation that things are working out just right for us."
zh: 在我脑海里我在想，“这就像我们是处于模拟中的另一个信号，事情对我们来说刚刚好。”

speaker: Unknown
en: What are the chances?
zh: 这几率有多大？

speaker: Unknown
en: The older I get, the more I believe that we are actually in a simulation.
zh: 我年纪越大，我就越相信我们实际上处于一个模拟中。

speaker: Unknown
en: The first time I heard that concept, I thought it was such an absurd concept.
zh: 我第一次听到这个概念时，我认为这是一个如此荒谬的概念。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now I'm like, "This might actually be happening."
zh: 现在我想，“这实际上可能正在发生。”

speaker: Unknown
en: You never know.
zh: 你永远不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: Following this thread, a lot of companies are trying to adjust to this new world.
zh: 沿着这条线索，很多公司正在试图适应这个新世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: You are doing an incredible job at actually doing this.
zh: 你在实际行动方面做得非常出色。

speaker: Unknown
en: We got connected through Kevin Weil, who is former CPO at OpenAI, now head of science at OpenAI.
zh: 我们通过 Kevin Weil 建立了联系，他是 OpenAI 的前 CPO，现任 OpenAI 的科学主管。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the way he described it is the work that you have done to turn Cisco the way he described it from an older, slower, more traditional enterprise to a very AI-forward company.
zh: 他描述的方式是，你所做的工作将 Cisco——正如他描述的那样——从一家较旧的、较慢的、更传统的企业转变为一家非常 AI 前瞻的公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: How many employees do you guys have?
zh: 你们有多少员工？

speaker: Unknown
en: You said 45,000.

speaker: Unknown
en: We have 90,000 employees, 43,000 watch the stream.
zh: 我们有 90,000 名员工，43,000 人观看了直播流。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, the big question for you is, it feels like it's really working, and this is very difficult to do at a company of that scale.
zh: 所以，对你来说最大的问题是，这感觉真的很有效，而且在这种规模的公司做到这一点非常困难。

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of leaders are trying to make it work.
zh: 很多领导者正在试图让它运作起来。

speaker: Unknown
en: What are two or three things that you've done that you think have been most impactful and effective in helping Cisco lean into AI, not be scared of it, and actually embrace the future?
zh: 你做过的哪两三件事是你认为最有影响力和最有效的，在帮助 Cisco 拥抱 AI，不害怕它，并真正拥抱未来？

speaker: Unknown
en: Innovation in my mind is a choice.
zh: 在我看来，创新是一种选择。

speaker: Unknown
en: I always find it interesting when people say, "Well, you're a large company, you can innovate.
zh: 当人们说，“嗯，你是一家大公司，你可以创新。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're a small company, you can innovate."
zh: 你是一家小公司，你可以创新。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, "No, it's just a choice.
zh: 就像是，“不，这只是一个选择。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every day you come into work and you can choose to be thinking about being creative, or you can choose to not be creative."
zh: 每天你来到工作中，你可以选择去思考发挥创意，或者你可以选择不去发挥创意。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like a little binary ... It's a binary choice You can make every hour, every minute of every day.
zh: 这就像是一个小小的二进制…… 这是一个二元选择，你在每天的每个小时、每分钟都可以做出的选择。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, we made that choice that says, Cisco is going to be not just an iconic company.
zh: 所以，我们做出了那个选择，即思科不仅要成为一家标志性公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: And Chuck Robbins, our CEO says this very eloquently.
zh: 我们的 CEO Chuck Robbins 非常雄辩地表达了这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's like, "I want Cisco not just to be an iconic company.
zh: 他像是说，“我希望思科不仅仅是一家标志性公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want Cisco to also be an iconic and innovative company."
zh: 我希望思科也要成为一家标志性和创新的公司。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, we got to make sure that we are actually innovating with the set of constraints that we are dealt with.
zh: 所以，我们必须确保在我们被赋予的一系列约束条件下，我们确实在进行创新。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every company has their own set of constraints, and we have our own set of constraints, and we have to make sure that given those constraints, we have to actually innovate really well.
zh: 每家公司都有自己的一套约束，我们有自己的一套约束，我们必须确保鉴于这些约束，我们必须真正地做好创新。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, what are the two or three things that have happened that have really helped us out?
zh: 那么，发生了哪两三件事真正帮助了我们？

speaker: Unknown
en: One was, being very clear on what is up for debate and what is not up for debate.
zh: 第一是，非常明确什么是可以争论的，什么是不可以争论的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because what can end up happening is you can always have a pocket veto in a large company where if you ask enough number of people, people say no.
zh: 因为最终可能发生的情况是，在大公司里你总是会遇到暗中否决，如果你问足够多的人，人们就会说不。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're a large company, you ask enough number of people, someone's going to say no, right?
zh: 如果你是一家大公司，你问了足够多的人，总会有人说不对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, when you have conviction about something that's happening, that is going to be a bet that you need to place.
zh: 所以，当你对正在发生的某事有信念时，这将是一个你需要下注的赌注。

speaker: Unknown
en: What most people think in large companies is large companies don't experiment.
zh: 大多数人在大公司里认为的是大公司不进行实验。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is in fact not true.
zh: 事实上并非如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: Large companies experiment a lot.
zh: 大公司经常进行实验。

speaker: Unknown
en: What large companies don't do is when an experiment works, they don't go all in and double down.
zh: 大公司不做的是当一个实验成功时，他们不会全力以赴并加倍下注。

speaker: Unknown
en: They try to keep hedging.
zh: 他们试图保持对冲。

speaker: Unknown
en: We didn't hedge on AI.
zh: 我们在 AI 上没有对冲。

speaker: Unknown
en: We said we were going to go all in.
zh: 我们说我们要全力以赴。

speaker: Unknown
en: That was number one.
zh: 这是第一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: What that meant was we had to get people to understand that their personal success and the success of the company are very aligned in us getting dexterous with the use of AI.
zh: 这意味着我们必须让人们明白，他们的个人成功和公司的成功在我们灵活使用 AI 方面是非常一致的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That means that if they feel like for some reason AI is going to take their job or AI is going to be negative for them, we had to reassure them that that was not the case.
zh: 这意味着如果他们觉得出于某种原因 AI 会抢走他们的工作，或者 AI 对他们不利，我们必须向他们保证情况并非如此。 [[127] 但反面情况是得到保证的，如果你不选择 AI，如果你在你的任何工作职能中不灵活，那么你的工作在这里长期来看可能就不会那么相关了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the reverse was guaranteed to be the case, that if you didn't choose AI, if you weren't going to be dexterous in whatever job function you're doing, then your job is probably not going to be that relevant over here in the long run.
zh: 但反过来是必然的，如果你不选择 AI，如果你在你的任何工作职能中不灵巧，那么你的工作在长远来看可能不会那么相关。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, that was the first thing that we did, that was a, I'm not a big fan of top-down hierarchy of going out and doing things.
zh: 所以，那是我们做的第一件事，那是，我不是自上而下的等级制度去做事的大粉丝。

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, deep down inside I don't respect hierarchy as much.
zh: 事实上，在内心深处我不那么尊重等级制度。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like it can constrain you at times.
zh: 我觉得它有时会束缚你。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I wanted to make ... On this one we were very, very deliberate.
zh: 但我想做…… 在这一点上我们非常、非常慎重。

speaker: Unknown
en: The entire company is on the same page, we are an AI-first company.
zh: 整个公司都在同一条战线上，我们是一家 AI 优先的公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this happened, we were working towards it even prior to ChatGPT, but ChatGPT became that seminal moment in November of '22 that we actually did that.
zh: 这件事发生了，我们在 ChatGPT 之前就为此努力，但 ChatGPT 成了 22 年 11 月的那个决定性时刻，我们实际上做到了这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, that was one.
zh: 所以，那是第一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Number two was we had to make sure that we defined what success looked like.
zh: 第二点是我们要确保定义了成功是什么样子的。

speaker: Unknown
en: The way that individual success was defined was everyone wanted to be a GM at Cisco.
zh: 个人成功被定义的方式是每个人都想成为思科的 GM。

speaker: Unknown
en: They wanted to own their own fiefdom, be a general manager.
zh: 他们想拥有自己的封地，成为总经理。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because they felt like, "In order for me to move up the ranks, I need to be a general manager, which means I need to have my own sales team.
zh: 因为他们觉得，“为了让我晋升，我需要成为一名总经理，这意味着我需要有自己的销售团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: I need to have my own marketing team.
zh: 我需要有自己的营销团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: I need to have my own product team.
zh: 我需要有自己的产品团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: I need to have my own engineering team.
zh: 我需要有自己的工程团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to make sure I run my own silo."
zh: 我要确保我运行自己的孤岛。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you're a 40 billion business in product revenues, 45 billion whatever we were at the time, and then all of a sudden your goal is that you're going to just run a bunch of $40 million businesses and break it up into a series of 40 million businesses, that's actually not a good thing for the company.
zh: 如果你是一家拥有 400 亿产品收入的企业，不管是 450 亿还是我们当时的那个数字，然后突然你的目标是你只是要运营一系列 4000 万美元的业务并将其分解为一系列 4000 万美元的业务，这对公司来说实际上不是一件好事。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, the thing we did was we said, "We have to become not a holding company of 251 acquisitions and thousands of different products, we have to become a platform company."
zh: 所以，我们要做的是我们说，“我们绝不能成为一家拥有 251 次收购和数千种不同产品的控股公司，我们必须成为一家平台公司。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And the characteristic of the platform is you have to be tightly integrated where the customer feels the same emotion, no matter what product of ours they use.
zh: 平台的特征是你必须紧密集成，以便无论客户使用我们的什么产品，他们都会感到同样的情感。 [[146] 有一套可以满足的相同期望，可靠性、信任、优雅和设计，以最有效的方式解决问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's the same set of expectations that can be served, reliability, trust, elegance and design, solving a problem in the most efficient way.
zh: 有一组同样的期望可以得到满足，可靠性、信任、优雅和设计，以最有效的方式解决问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Those are the things we want to strive to do.
zh: 这些是我们努力要做到的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: But you don't have to buy everything all at once, because we also want to be realistic about the fact that not every customer only uses Cisco top to bottom.
zh: 但你不必一次性购买所有东西，因为我们也想现实一点，事实上并非每个客户只从头到尾使用思科。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's an ecosystem.
zh: 这里有一个生态系统。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, loosely coupled, but tightly integrated.
zh: 所以，松散耦合，但紧密集成。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't have to buy everything all at once, but boy, when you do buy two things together, they work like magic.
zh: 你不必一次性买下所有东西，但是，当你把两样东西买在一起时，它们运作起来像魔法一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, that was the second big thing we did.
zh: 所以，那是我们做的第二件大事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the third one we did was we said, "Let's make sure that we have a mental model shift in the company."
zh: 然后我们做的第三件事是，我们说，“让我们确保公司在思维模式上有所转变。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And we did this about five, five and a half years ago when I first joined.
zh: 我们在大约五年半前我刚加入时做了这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: This was a very deliberate decision, which was, we cannot operate in a walled garden.
zh: 这是一个非常深思熟虑的决定，即我们不能在一个围墙花园中运作。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have to make sure that we operate in an open ecosystem, which means we have to be completely comfortable with having a competitor that we're going to partner with.
zh: 我们必须确保我们在一个开放的生态系统中运作，这意味着我们必须完全坦然地与我们将要合作的竞争对手共存。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's okay.
zh: 这是可以的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't have to think about this in a zero-sum manner.
zh: 我们不必以零和的方式思考这个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: In order for me to win, someone has to lose.
zh: 为了让我赢，必须有人输。

speaker: Unknown
en: We can partner, because if a customer has made a choice of going with company A and company B and we happen to be one of those two companies, we owe it to the customer to invest in their success in that other company because if the customer succeeds, that success has a flow through rate to you that's going to be pretty high.
zh: 我们可以合作，因为如果客户选择了 A 公司和 B 公司，而我们恰好是这两家公司之一，我们就欠客户一份对那另一家公司成功的投入，因为如果客户成功了，这种成功会有相当高的流向率传递给你。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, that's what we did, and I think that's been those principles of building great products, but making sure that it operates like a platform and having an open ecosystem, I think has been kind of central.
zh: 所以，这就是我们所做的，我认为这些原则就是构建伟大的产品，但要确保它像一个平台一样运作，并拥有一个开放的生态系统，我认为这一直是核心所在。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then not being confused about the fact that we'll be AI-first from the top down.
zh: 不要混淆这一点，我们将自上而下地以 AI 为先。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to take a tangent and make sure people understand what Cisco even does these days.
zh: 我想稍微岔开一下话题，确保大家理解 Cisco 如今到底是做什么的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think as a lay person, you'll think about Cisco and you're like, "Okay, they WebEx, yes.
zh: 我认为作为一个外行，你会想到 Cisco，你会说，“好吧，他们有 WebEx，是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: They make maybe some routers."
zh: 他们可能制造一些路由器。”

speaker: Unknown
en: You guys are key to this massive AI infrastructure build-out that's happening right now.
zh: 你们是当前正在发生的这场大规模 AI 基础设施建设的关键角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're a major player in this.
zh: 你们是其中的主要参与者。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think people realize this, people listening to this podcast.
zh: 我认为人们没有意识到这一点，收听这个播客的人们。

speaker: Unknown
en: Give us just a quick glimpse into how Cisco fits into this massive build-out and just what does Cisco these days?
zh: 请给我们快速瞥一眼 Cisco 是如何融入这场大规模建设的，以及 Cisco 如今究竟是做什么的？

speaker: Unknown
en: Cisco is a critical infrastructure company for the AI era.
zh: Cisco 是 AI 时代的关键基础设施公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: What does that mean?
zh: 这是什么意思？

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you think about where the constraints are right now, if you think that AI is going to be one of the biggest movements, and then you ask yourself the question, "What could hold AI back?"
zh: 但是如果你考虑一下目前的制约因素，如果你认为 AI 将是最大的潮流之一，然后你问自己一个问题，“什么东西可能会阻碍 AI？”

speaker: Unknown
en: There's three things where we feel like we can have a direct impact that can hold AI back.
zh: 有三个领域，我们觉得我们可以产生直接影响，而这些领域可能会阻碍 AI。

speaker: Unknown
en: Number one is there's an infrastructure constraint.
zh: 第一个是存在基础设施约束。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's just not enough power compute and network bandwidth in the world to go out and satiate the needs of AI.
zh: 世界上根本没有足够的电力算力和网络带宽来满足 AI 的需求。

speaker: Unknown
en: Number two is, there's a trust deficit.
zh: 第二个是，存在信任赤字。

speaker: Unknown
en: If people don't trust these systems, they're not going to use them.
zh: 如果人们不信任这些系统，他们就不会使用它们。

speaker: Unknown
en: And right now there's a lot of mistrust in these systems.
zh: 而目前对这些系统存在很多不信任。

speaker: Unknown
en: Hallucination is a feature when you're writing poetry, but when you're trying to go out and run predictable systems, hallucination can be a bad thing.
zh: 当你在写诗时，幻觉是一个特性，但当你试图运行可预测的系统时，幻觉可能是一件坏事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And these models are unpredictable, they're non-deterministic, and so they have to make sure that they have safety and security factored into them.
zh: 而且这些模型是不可预测的，它们是非确定性的，因此必须确保其中包含了安全性和安全性因素。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the third area is a data gap.
zh: 第三个领域是数据鸿沟。

speaker: Unknown
en: So far we've trained these models with human-generated data publicly available on the internet, but we are running out of human-generated data publicly available on the internet to train the models.
zh: 到目前为止，我们使用互联网上公开可用的人类生成的数据来训练这些模型，但我们的互联网上公开可用的人类生成数据正耗尽，无法再用来训练模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: And every company is going to differentiate based on their own proprietary enterprise data being used to train the models, synthetic data and machine data, which is where the most amount of growth is.
zh: 每家公司都将根据用于训练模型的自有专有企业数据、合成数据和机器数据来实现差异化，而这正是增长最旺盛的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the third category of machine data we can play a massive role in at Cisco.
zh: 在 Cisco，我们可以在这第三类机器数据中发挥巨大作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, what does Cisco do then?
zh: 那么，Cisco 到底是做什么的呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: If you think about a GPU, which is what everyone now is very clear because of the great job that Jensen has done that here's what a GPU's core contribution is to AI.
zh: 如果你想到一个 GPU，由于 Jensen 出色的工作，现在每个人都非常清楚这是 GPU 对 AI 的核心贡献。

speaker: Unknown
en: If these GPUs aren't networked together, you don't have AI, because it used to be that you could train a model on a single GPU, but then what happened was the model got too big to be put on a single GPU.
zh: 如果这些 GPU 没有联网在一起，你就没有 AI，因为过去你可以在单个 GPU 上训练一个模型，但后来发生的事情是模型变得太大，无法放在单个 GPU 上。

speaker: Unknown
en: So then you had a server with eight GPUs that got connected together.
zh: 所以你有了一个连接在一起的服务器，里面有八个 GPU。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, you could train a model with eight GPUs.
zh: 所以，你可以用八个 GPU 训练一个模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: But then that wasn't good enough.
zh: 但那还不够好。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, then what happened was you said, "I'm going to have a rack of servers that I'm going to network together."
zh: 所以，后来发生的事情是你说，“我要有一机架的服务器，我要把它们联网在一起。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That at some point wasn't big enough.
zh: 在某些时候那也不够大。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so then they said, "I'm going to have a cluster of racks that are connected together."
zh: 然后他们说，“我要有一 cluster 的机架连接在一起。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And that connected together is the operative word.
zh: 这个连接在一起是关键词。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's what we end up doing is NVIDIA makes the GPUs and we connect those GPUs together.
zh: 我们最终所做的是，NVIDIA 制造 GPU，而我们连接这些 GPU。

speaker: Unknown
en: AMD makes the GPUs, we connect them together.
zh: AMD 制造 GPU，我们连接它们。

speaker: Unknown
en: And now what's happened, Lenny, is you have these data centers that might be hundreds of kilometers apart that need to operate like one coherent cluster, which means that they're completely in sync.
zh: 现在发生的事情是，Lenny，你有这些可能相距数百公里的数据中心，它们需要像一个连贯的集群一样运作，这意味着它们完全同步。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every GPU is in sync with each other when you're doing a training run.
zh: 当你进行训练运行时，每个 GPU 都彼此同步。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that requires a very sophisticated set of technologies that we build to make sure that you could have two data centers, 800 kilometers apart, but boy, they run completely in sync with each other.
zh: 这需要一套非常复杂的技术，我们要构建这些技术，以确保你可以有两个相距 800 公里的数据中心，但是，它们运行起来完全同步。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's what Cisco does.
zh: 这就是思科所做的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We provide the networking, we provide the optics technology, we provide the safety and security technology, we provide the observability, we provide the data platform, all of those things together for making sure that we provide critical infrastructure for the AI era.
zh: 我们提供网络,我们提供光学技术,我们提供安全技术,我们提供可观测性,我们提供数据平台,所有这些东西结合在一起,以确保我们为 AI 时代提供关键基础设施。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, being on the inside of this massive investment that is happening across the world, what do you think isn't being priced in into where things are heading into how much life will change, or just the scale of this build out?
zh: 那么,身处这场正在全球发生的巨额投资的核心,你认为哪些因素没有被计入未来的走向,无论是生活将发生多大的改变,还是仅仅是这个建设规模的巨大?

speaker: Unknown
en: Years ago I'd had a chance to meet with Ray Kurzweil.
zh: 几年前我有过机会和 Ray Kurzweil 会面。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's the chief scientist at Google for a while, and I think he still is.
zh: 他是谷歌的首席科学家已经有一段时间了,我想他现在还是。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he had talked about, he was writing this book called Live Long Enough to Live Forever.
zh: 他谈到了,他当时正在写一本叫《Live Long Enough to Live Forever》的书。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I was talking to him, I'm like, "What is the impact to human population if all of a sudden you can have 15 generations living simultaneously, because we have an indefinite span of life?
zh: 所以我和他交谈,我说,"如果突然间你可以有 15 代人同时生活,因为我们的寿命无限期延长,这对人类人口的影响是什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: Because now all of a sudden everything has to change.
zh: 因为现在突然间一切都不得不改变。

speaker: Unknown
en: How does housing work, housing work?
zh: 住房怎么运作,住房怎么运作?

speaker: Unknown
en: How does agriculture work?
zh: 农业怎么运作?

speaker: Unknown
en: How does transportation work?
zh: 交通怎么运作?

speaker: Unknown
en: How does ... Everything changes?"
zh: 怎么... 一切都在改变?"

speaker: Unknown
en: And he looked at me and he had the most profound answer and he said, "Most people can't think exponentially, because they always think exponentially maybe on a single dimension."
zh: 他看着我,他给出了最深刻的回答,他说,"大多数人无法进行指数级思考,因为他们总是可能只在单一维度上进行指数级思考。"

speaker: Unknown
en: But what ends up happening in these things is you can sometimes you have to keep in mind that exponentiality happens across multiple dimensions all at once.
zh: 但在这些事情中最终发生的是,有时候你必须牢记,指数级增长同时在多个维度上发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, if you do have an indefinite span of life, you have to assume that humans are creative enough that they're going to find a way to have a three-day crop cycle.
zh: 所以,如果你真的拥有无限期的寿命,你必须假设人类足够有创造力,他们会找到一种方法来实现三天一轮的作物周期。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they probably will have 5,000 story skyscrapers, and there will be a bunch of things in society that we have assumed are not solvable that'll now be solvable.
zh: 而且他们可能会有 5000 层的摩天大楼,并且社会中将有一堆我们原本认为无法解决的事情,现在变得可解决了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, when you go back to your question and say, what changes in this entire equation that has not been factored in well?
zh: 所以,当你回到你的问题说,在这个整个方程式中,什么变化没有被很好地纳入考虑?

speaker: Unknown
en: I think today AI is looked at largely as a productivity tool and an aggregation mechanism.
zh: 我认为今天 AI 在很大程度上被视为一种生产力工具和一种聚合机制。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have data all over and I'm going to be able to make sure that language can be used to compose the data in a way that I can give you, Lenny, the answer to the question that you're looking for that I think is the 0.0001% of the tip of the iceberg.
zh: 我到处都有数据,我将能够确保语言可以被用来组成数据,以便我可以给你,Lenny,你正在寻找的问题的答案,我认为这只是冰山一角的 0.0001%。

speaker: Unknown
en: The reality is, is we will have original insights generated that don't exist in the human corpus of knowledge, and we will have the physical world get augmented to language where capacity is augmented to humans.
zh: 现实是,我们将产生不存在于人类知识语料库中的原创洞察,并且我们将拥有物理世界被增强到语言,即容量被增强给人类。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what we have to be careful of is that that capacity is working on behalf of humans, but if that capacity is augmented to humans, you can now do things that you really care to do, and not do things that you don't care to do.
zh: 我们必须小心的是,这种能力是为人类工作的,但如果这种能力被增强给人类,你现在就可以做你真正关心的事情,而不做你不关心的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so our biggest realization that we had when we were using Codex, for example, when we were writing a code with OpenAI's kind of model and development tool, was the first three months we were screwing around with this.
zh: 所以当我们使用 Codex 时,我们最大的一个领悟是,例如,当我们用 OpenAI 的那种模型和开发工具编写代码时,前三个月我们在搞这个东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then there was this light bulb that runoffs.
zh: 然后那个灯泡亮了。

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, there was a former deployed engineer from OpenAI that told us about this, which is, "Hey, stop trying to think of this as a tool.
zh: 事实上,有一位 OpenAI 的前部署工程师告诉了我们这一点,也就是,"嘿,别再试图把它当成一个工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: Think of this as a teammate that got added to your team, and your framing will change, and the way in which you actually use the technology will change."
zh: 把它想象成一个被添加到你的团队的队友,你的框架会改变,你实际使用技术的方式也会改变。"

speaker: Unknown
en: And that essentially, if you compound that to how society operates, that's going to be pretty profound as an implication, while keeping in mind that these safety and security risks are non-trivial and they're real.
zh: 而本质上,如果你将其复合到社会的运作方式中,这将是一个非常深刻的影响,同时要牢记这些安全风险是不可忽视的,它们是真实的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you can't be completely flippant about them, because how an AI identifies its own success and its own ambition will really matter.
zh: 你不能完全对它们不屑一顾,因为 AI 如何识别它自己的成功和它自己的野心将非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we have to make sure that we actually keep guardrails around that, because it is in service of humans, it is not to go out and build a society by itself.
zh: 我们必须确保我们实际上在周围设置护栏,因为它是服务于人类的,它不是要自己去建立一个社会。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I do think that those are important kind of checks and balances you have to keep in mind.
zh: 我确实认为这些是重要的你必须记住的制衡机制。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the thing that people sometimes miss out in this very polarized narrative, which is we are either going to have nothing to do in society, or this is going to be completely useless as a piece of technology.
zh: 但是人们在这个非常两极分化的叙述中有时会忽略的一点是,我们要么将在社会中无事可做,要么这项技术将完全无用。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's not a helpful narrative.
zh: 我认为这不是一个有益的叙述。

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, what is helpful is saying, "As we reconstruct society for the next phase, how can we make sure that life gets infinitely better?
zh: 事实上,有帮助的是说,"当我们为社会重建下一阶段时,我们如何确保生活变得无限美好?

speaker: Unknown
en: How can we make sure that diseases get solved?
zh: 我们如何确保疾病得到解决?

speaker: Unknown
en: How can we make sure that poverty gets eradicated?
zh: 我们如何确保贫困被根除?

speaker: Unknown
en: How can we make sure that how people learn and find excitement and joy out of life gets compounded meaningfully?"
zh: 我们如何确保人们学习和从生活中找到兴奋和快乐的方式得到有意义的复合增长?"

speaker: Unknown
en: If that happens, I think there's goodness that comes out of this.
zh: 如果这种情况发生,我认为就会从中产生好处。

speaker: Unknown
en: A line that I often think about is, Huang has had this thought that the best case scenario with AI, because he was a very AI doomer for a long time, and I think the reason he got leaned into AI is like, "I need to help steer this in a direction that isn't going to harm the world."
zh: 我经常想到的一句话是,Huang 有过这种想法,即 AI 的最佳情况,因为他长期以来是一个 AI 厄运论者,我认为他投身 AI 的原因就像,"我需要帮助引导它走向一个不会伤害世界的方向。"

speaker: Unknown
en: The way he described it is, "The best case scenario for humanity is we're the house cat, where AI is just like, 'Okay, nice.
zh: 他描述的方式是,"对人类来说最好的情况是我们是家猫,AI 就像,'好的,不错。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just keep sitting here with me and I'll take care of you.'"
zh: 就坐在这里陪我,我会照顾你。'"

speaker: Unknown
en: But by the way, the things that he is doing right now are nothing short of extraordinary.
zh: 但顺便说一句,他现在正在做的事情简直非凡。

speaker: Unknown
en: And for all the critique that one can have, the level of deep thinking that's going on with his company, it's just crazy.
zh: 尽管人们可以提出各种批评,但在他的公司里正在进行的那种深度思考的程度,简直疯了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, as you've been thinking about where things are heading, I've been liking to ask this question with people with kids, is there anything you're kind of shifting in how you raise your daughter, keeping in mind where things are heading?
zh: 那么,当你一直在思考事情的发展方向时,我一直喜欢向有孩子的人问这个问题,考虑到事情的发展方向,你在抚养女儿方面有什么改变吗?

speaker: Unknown
en: Are there skills you're trying to instill in her values you're trying to instill in her that will help her thrive in this future?
zh: 有什么技能你试图灌输给她,有什么价值观你试图灌输给她,这将帮助她在未来中茁壮成长?

speaker: Unknown
en: We made a choice, and I didn't know how that choice was going to go.
zh: 我们做了一个选择,我不知道那个选择会走向何方。

speaker: Unknown
en: That was actually not even an active choice, it was a passive choice.
zh: 那实际上甚至不是一个主动的选择,而是一个被动的选择。

speaker: Unknown
en: Frankly, even might have been slightly intellectually lazy in the way that we did it, but it actually worked out pretty well in the sense that we didn't really deprive her of the use of technology.
zh: 坦率地说,甚至可能在我们做这件事的方式上稍微有点智力懒惰,但实际上结果还不错,因为我们的确没有真正剥夺她使用技术的权利。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a school of thought that says, "Keep technology away from the kids for a while."
zh: 有一种思想流派说,"让孩子远离技术一段时间。"

speaker: Unknown
en: We didn't do that.
zh: 我们没有那样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: And frankly, I didn't know how it was going to work out, because there are things about the way that the generation is, and by the way, all of us, not just new generation, but this kind of constantly being glued to your phone all the time and not being able to actually put that down and have a conversation.
zh: 坦率地说,我不知道结果会如何,因为这一代人的方式有一些东西,而且顺便说一句,我们所有人,不仅仅是新一代,而是这种一直黏在手机上,无法真正放下手机进行交谈。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's an important skill in humans to have and preserve over time.
zh: 我认为这是人类拥有并随着时间保留的一项重要技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in fact, as AI does more for us, we should be able to have more of this time.
zh: 事实上，随着 AI 为我们做得更多，我们应该能够拥有更多这种时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't have to worry about every notification that's coming on my phone every minute of the day, because maybe I can be more present in the moment that I'm in.
zh: 我不必担心每天每分钟我手机上弹出的每一个通知，因为也许我可以更专注于我当下的时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: She just turned 15 and the night before she was turning 15, what I realized is she is so emotionally mature.
zh: 她刚满 15 岁，在她 15 岁的前一天晚上，我意识到的是她的情感非常成熟。

speaker: Unknown
en: We were sitting down one night and she's like, "Hey dad, just so you know, I feel really good right now about having a very strong value system."
zh: 一天晚上我们坐在一起，她就像说，“嘿爸爸，只是让你知道，我现在感觉很好，因为我有一个非常强大的价值体系。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "Oh, okay, what does that mean?
zh: 我就像说，“噢，好的，那是什么意思？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And say more."
zh: “多说点。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And she's like, "Well, can you name five things that you feel so convicted about that if the entire world disagreed with you," this is the day before she's turning 15, okay?
zh: 她就像说，“那么，你能说出五件你确信的事情吗？哪怕全世界都不同意你，”这是她 15 岁的前一天，好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: "The entire world disagreed with you.
zh: “全世界都不同意你。

speaker: Unknown
en: You would still feel like you were right on that and that would waver you."
zh: 你仍然会觉得你是正确的，而那会使你动摇。”

speaker: Unknown
en: She's like, "I have a certain core set of things that I believe in where I am completely confident that if everyone disagreed with me, I'm still good."
zh: 她就像说，“我有某些核心的信仰，我是完全自信的，即使每个人都不同意我，我依然很好。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, by the way, I have to kind of coach her on the, "Hey, when you get new data, be open-minded to changing your mind."
zh: 现在，顺便说一句，我不得不在某种程度上指导她，“嘿，当你获得新数据时，要心态开放地改变你的想法。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But it was actually a very interesting dynamic, which is, if we can have them be exposed to technology but have the right value system, you might actually have the best of both worlds.
zh: 但这实际上是一个非常有趣的动态，也就是，如果我们能让她们接触技术但拥有正确的价值体系，你可能实际上两全其美。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the day ain't over yet.
zh: 而且这一天还没结束呢。

speaker: Unknown
en: She's 15.
zh: 她才 15 岁。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a lot of chances for her getting influenced by external factors and all of that.
zh: 她有很多机会受到外部因素的影响等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: But what you have to do is make sure that you instill the right values, but then also expose them to the reality of what the world is today and not completely insulate them from that.
zh: 但你必须做的是确保你灌输正确的价值观，但也要让她们接触到当今世界的现实，而不是完全将她们与现实隔绝。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, the way that it worked out, it did end up working well, and we were lucky for no credit to us she was able to use technology to get her EQ higher and higher, and we were lucky on that front.
zh: 所以，事情的结果是，最终确实运作得很好，我们很幸运，不归功于我们，她能够利用技术来提高她的情商，我们在那方面很幸运。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we know it can go sideways the other way too.
zh: 我们知道它也可能向另一个方向发展。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I do feel like right now, at least for my one daughter, what we try to do is get her exposed to the technology, but make sure that we focus a lot more on the values that we need to have that govern us on a day-to-day basis.
zh: 但我确实觉得现在，至少对于我的一个女儿来说，我们试图做的是让她接触技术，但要确保我们更多地关注我们需要拥有的、支配我们日常生活的价值观。

speaker: Unknown
en: Kindness, not being arrogant, hard work, work ethic, those things matter.
zh: 善良，不傲慢，努力工作，职业道德，这些事情很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: And those are timeless in my mind.
zh: 在我看来，这些是永恒的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think those change, because take risks, be creative, that kind of stuff.
zh: 我不认为这些会改变，因为要冒险，要有创造力，诸如此类。

speaker: Unknown
en: Jeetu, these are parenting goals.
zh: Jeetu，这些是育儿目标。

speaker: Unknown
en: As I hear this, I have a two and a half year old Ms, it sounds like you've done an amazing job raising your daughter.
zh: 当我听到这个时，我有一个两岁半的女儿，听起来你在抚养女儿方面做得非常出色。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would take zero credit for it.
zh: 我不居功。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think she deserves a lot of credit for growing up to be who she's become.
zh: 我认为她值得很多赞誉，成长为她现在的样子。

speaker: Unknown
en: And her mother.
zh: 还有她的母亲。

speaker: Unknown
en: Got to shout out mom.
zh: 必须要为妈妈欢呼。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's interesting is that I know Anthropic is really big, this idea of values and just how you operate.
zh: 有趣的是，我知道 Anthropic 非常看重价值观这个理念以及你的运作方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Anthropic has this constitution they released of how the values essentially of Claude.
zh: Anthropic 发布了一部宪法，本质上阐述了 Claude 的价值观。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's so interesting how much similarity there is to how to raise a great person and to how to steer an AI correctly.
zh: 有趣的是，培养一个优秀的人和正确引导 AI 之间有多少相似之处。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's right.
zh: 没错。

speaker: Unknown
en: And by the way, some of your beliefs and your system around you might change, but values tend to be pretty long lasting.
zh: 顺便说一句，你的一些信念和你的系统可能会改变，但价值观往往是相当持久的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And culture and a company tends to be pretty long lasting.
zh: 公司的文化往往是相当持久的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Ben Horowitz talks about this very eloquently.
zh: Ben Horowitz 非常雄辩地谈到了这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: The culture is just a set of norms that accompany action.
zh: 文化只是伴随着行动的一套规范。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not a set of beliefs, it's a set of behaviors that you exude within the company.
zh: 它不是一套信念，它是你在公司内表现出的一套行为。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's actually very, very true, because when things aren't going right, how do people behave to go solve problems and come together?
zh: 这实际上是非常非常真实的，因为当事情进展不顺利时，人们如何表现去解决问题并团结在一起？

speaker: Unknown
en: And that actually forms your cultural norms.
zh: 那实际上形成了你的文化规范。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think those cultural norms, it's very important to be intentional about it.
zh: 我认为这些文化规范，非常重要的是要有意图地去建立它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And as you have more automation in the world, being intentional not just with humans but also with machines and what you want to do to create the guardrails, I think is pretty important.
zh: 随着世界上有更多的自动化，不仅对人类要有意图，对机器也是如此，以及你想做什么来创建护栏，我认为这是相当重要的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to take us in a different direction.
zh: 我要带我们换个方向。

speaker: Unknown
en: I talked to Aaron Levie, your former boss at Box.
zh: 我和 Aaron Levie 谈过，你在 Box 的前老板。

speaker: Unknown
en: Dear, dear friend.
zh: 亲爱的朋友。

speaker: Unknown
en: And friend.
zh: 也是朋友。

speaker: Unknown
en: I asked him just what should I ask you about with something that he learned from you that has stuck with him ever since working with you?
zh: 我问他关于你的事，他说自从和你共事以来，有什么他从你那里学到并且一直铭记在心的事情？

speaker: Unknown
en: And he share this concept of the right to win, which he says has informed the way he thinks about strategy ever since.
zh: 他分享了这个获胜权的概念，他说这影响了他在战略方面的思维方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about what this is and how folks might use this when they're thinking about product strategy, company strategy?
zh: 谈谈这是什么，以及人们在思考产品战略、公司战略时如何使用它？

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the things that we would always talk about is in the areas that we are going to participate, do we have permission to play?
zh: 我们经常谈论的一件事是，在我们要参与的领域，我们有参与许可吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Every company has to make sure that the way in which they provide points of insertion and logical entry into a market is a lot of times dependent on, do you have the permission to play in that market?
zh: 每家公司都必须确保其提供插入点和逻辑进入市场的方式，很多时候取决于，你有在这个市场玩的许可吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you have an avenue to have a route to market to be able to take that product?
zh: 你有一条途径拥有通往市场的路线,能够带走那个产品吗?

speaker: Unknown
en: Just by building a product that is amazing in some area, you don't end up actually getting it to mass scale distribution.
zh: 仅仅通过构建一个在某方面很棒的产品,你实际上最终无法让它达到大规模分发。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, one of the things that we would always do is ask ourselves a question.
zh: 因此,我们要做的一件事就是总是问自己一个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: "We're building this new category or we're building this new capability.
zh: “我们正在构建这个新类别或者我们正在构建这个新功能。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it going to be logical for people that Box built it versus another company building it?
zh: 如果是 Box 构建了它而不是另一家公司构建了它,这对人们来说会是合乎逻辑的吗?

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it going to be logical for people that Cisco built it versus another company building it?"
zh: 如果是 Cisco 构建了它而不是另一家公司构建了它,这对人们来说会是合乎逻辑的吗?”

speaker: Unknown
en: So, that's this notion of permission to play, the right to win.
zh: 所以,这就是参与许可的概念,获胜的权利。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do we have a right to win in that area because we have permission to play?
zh: 我们在那个领域拥有获胜的权利是因为我们拥有参与许可吗?

speaker: Unknown
en: And do we have the route to market to be able to take that product and get it to mass scale distribution?
zh: 并且我们拥有通往市场的路线能够带走那个产品并让它达到大规模分发吗?

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you can do those things right, then actually your dollars that you expend on building product actually have an outsized return.
zh: 如果你能把这些事情做对,那么实际上你在构建产品上花费的美元实际上会有超额回报。

speaker: Unknown
en: If not, then you can actually end up spending a lot of money on product.
zh: 如果不能,那么你实际上最终可能会在产品上花很多钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: Where the product people think, "Ah, these sales guys don't get it.
zh: 产品人员会想,“啊,这些销售家伙不懂。

speaker: Unknown
en: They don't know how to sell it, especially in enterprise software."
zh: 他们不知道怎么卖掉它,尤其是在企业软件中。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And sales people think, "These product guys don't get it.
zh: 而销售人员认为,“这些产品家伙不懂。

speaker: Unknown
en: They don't know how to build it."
zh: 他们不知道怎么构建它。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, I think in order to stop that, what you have to do is you have to actually use your scale as an advantage and you have to use the areas where you've got the ability to have permission to play where people feel like this is very logical for a company like Cisco.
zh: 所以,我认为为了阻止这种情况,你必须做的是你必须实际上利用你的规模作为优势,并且你必须利用你拥有参与许可的领域,在那里人们觉得这对于像 Cisco 这样的公司来说是非常合乎逻辑的。

speaker: Unknown
en: When we say we are going to network the GPUs and make sure that we actually have a trusted system in AI, that is not far-fetched for someone to go out and think about, because it's a very natural thing for us to do, because for the past 40 years we've been doing it for the rest of the infrastructure that was not AI.
zh: 当说我们要将 GPU 联网并确保我们实际上在 AI 中拥有一个可信的系统时,对于某人来说去思考这个问题并非牵强,因为这对我们来说是一件非常自然的事情,因为在过去的 40 年里我们一直为其余的非 AI 基础设施做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, that's not a far cry to say, "Okay, we'll now do it for AI."
zh: 所以,这并非天壤之别来说,“好吧,我们现在将为 AI 做这件事。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that was an area that Aaron and I spent a fair ... And by the way, I'm glad that he took that out of me.
zh: 我认为这是 Aaron 和我花费了相当...顺便说一下,我很高兴他从我身上把它拿出来了。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's so much I've learned from him.
zh: 我从他那里学到了很多东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: The biggest area I've learned from him is, you never give up.
zh: 我从他那里学到的最大的领域是,你从不放弃。

speaker: Unknown
en: And persistence beats intellect, and stamina beats intellect any day of the week, twice on Sunday.
zh: 毅力战胜智力,耐力战胜智力,一周中的任何一天,周日加倍。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that guy is as smart as they come, but that's not the biggest reason he's successful.
zh: 那个家伙像他们来的一样聪明,但这不是他成功的最大原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: The biggest reason he's successful is he has an enormous amount of staying power in the game.
zh: 他成功的最大原因是他在这场游戏中拥有巨大的持久力。

speaker: Unknown
en: Going back to my daughter's comment of no matter what everyone else says, his convictions and belief, he will actually stick by them and actually get through the hardest times.
zh: 回到我女儿的评论,不管其他人说什么,他的信念和信仰,他实际上会坚持它们并实际上度过最艰难的时期。

speaker: Unknown
en: I totally believe that.
zh: 我完全相信这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like I'm not the smartest person in the room usually, and I succeed in large part because I just work really hard.
zh: 我觉得我通常不是房间里最聪明的人,我成功很大程度上是因为我只是真的很努力工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're pretty smart though.
zh: 你虽然相当聪明。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've been watching your podcast for a while.
zh: 我看你的播客有一段时间了。

speaker: Unknown
en: You've done a pretty amazing job.
zh: 你做得非常出色。

speaker: Unknown
en: I appreciate it.
zh: 我很感激。

speaker: Unknown
en: In this permission to win concept, the reason I think it's so important is it's so easy to build stuff now.
zh: 在这个获胜许可概念中,我认为它如此重要的原因是现在构建东西太容易了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone's just building, building, building, launching, launching, launching.
zh: 每个人都只是在构建,构建,构建,发布,发布,发布。

speaker: Unknown
en: It feels like this is an increasingly important lever is why will we win in this space?
zh: 感觉这是一个越来越重要的杠杆,为什么我们将在这个空间获胜?

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm curious if there's an example you can share either from Box or Cisco where it's just like, "Okay, this is like we're going to do this because we have the permission to play here."
zh: 我很好奇你是否可以分享一个例子,无论是从 Box 还是 Cisco,就像这样,“好吧,这就是我们要做这件事,因为我们在这里拥有参与许可。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I agree with you in the sense that if generating code is something that becomes abundant, that doesn't mean you're going to have better technology just because you can generate a lot of code.
zh: 我在某种意义上同意你,如果生成代码是一种变得丰富的东西,这并不意味着你仅仅因为你能生成大量代码就会拥有更好的技术。

speaker: Unknown
en: You still need human judgment, you still need a level of intuition on what problems are the right ones to solve.
zh: 你仍然需要人类的判断力,你仍然需要一定程度的直觉来知道哪些问题是正确的问题要去解决。

speaker: Unknown
en: And yes, AI can help you with all of that, but it's not something that ... That's where humans have a superpower, they have instinct, and they can actually make sure that they can fulfill out a vision that says, "This is what I think this could be in the fullness of time."
zh: 是的,AI 可以在所有这些方面帮助你,但这不是某种...那是人类拥有超能力的地方,他们拥有本能,并且他们实际上可以确保他们能够实现一个愿景,说“我认为这在充分的时间里会是这样的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, that I think is pretty important.
zh: 所以,我认为这非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, the easier it gets for us to get the bottlenecks out to generate code, the harder it gets for us to make sure that there's not AI slop in the market and that we actually are very selective on what are the things that are going to be the most important things that solve the most important problems moving forward?
zh: 所以,对我们来说,消除瓶颈来生成代码变得越容易,我们要确保市场上没有 AI 污垢并且我们要非常有选择性什么是最重要的事情来解决最重要的困难向前推进,这件事就变得越困难?

speaker: Unknown
en: Example of permission to play is, I mean, there's so many ideas that a company the size of Cisco, we have constantly new ideas that keep coming up.
zh: 参与许可的例子是,我的意思是,有如此多的想法,对于一个像 Cisco 这样规模的公司,我们要有不断的新想法出现。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then in those new ideas that keep coming up, people will always say, "Oh my goodness, this company is doing so well.
zh: 然后在这些不断出现的新想法中,人们总是会说,“天哪,这家公司做得这么好。

speaker: Unknown
en: We should just go into that market, or we should just go into this market."
zh: 我们应该进入那个市场,或者我们应该进入这个市场。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And 90% of the times, 99% of the times, I find myself saying no.
zh: 而 90% 的时候,99% 的时候,我发现自己在说不。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the reason for that is you have to be extremely selective of where you expend your calories.
zh: 原因是你必须对你花费热量的地方极其挑剔。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that caloric expenditure is where if you expend your calories in a very focused way, the results you'll get from that focus area tend to be outsized and disproportionate.
zh: 这种热量消耗是,如果你以非常集中的方式花费你的热量,你从那个集中领域获得的结果往往是超额的和不成比例的。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you dissipate that caloric burn across multitude of different areas, nothing gets enough girth to be able to go out and drive it all the way through.
zh: 如果你在许多不同的领域分散这种热量燃烧,没有任何东西能获得足够的周长来能够一路到底驱动它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, why are we not in business-to-consumer tech at Cisco?
zh: 那么,为什么我们在 Cisco 不做面向消费者的技术?

speaker: Unknown
en: Why are we not going out and building things that are very very B2C?
zh: 为什么我们不出去构建非常非常 B2C 的东西?

speaker: Unknown
en: Because I don't think we have a distribution channel that actually is within our DNA.
zh: 因为我认为我们没有一个实际上在我们 DNA 内的分发渠道。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think that we've got permission to play there.
zh: 我不认为我们有获准在那里运营。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's an area where it would be extremely hard for people to growth that Cisco should be the one who's participating in that.
zh: 在那个领域，人们很难去发展，Cisco 应该是那个参与其中的方。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now can we do it?
zh: 我们能做吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Of course we can do it.
zh: 我们当然能做。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that where we want to go or do we want to go where there's so much opportunity in the areas where we can actually prosecute with the ability to operate from a position of strength that you'll just get a much better return for the dollar that you invest.
zh: 那是我们想去的地方吗，还是我们想去有这么多机会的地方，在我们实际上有能力从优势地位运作的领域，你将为你投资的每一美元获得好得多的回报。

speaker: Unknown
en: You mentioned Aaron as a CEO that you learned a lot from.
zh: 你提到了 Aaron，说你从他这位 CEO 那里学到了很多。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm curious what other CEOs you've learned a lot from and what's something you learned from them?
zh: 我很好奇你还从其他哪些 CEO 那里学到了很多，以及你从他们身上学到了什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Chuck Robbins is one of my favorite humans.
zh: Chuck Robbins 是我最喜欢的人类之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And not just because I work for him, I work for him because he's one of my favorite humans.
zh: 不仅仅是因为我为他工作，我为他工作是因为他是我最喜欢的人类之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what I've learned from him, he had this kind of great line.
zh: 我从他那里学到的，他有这句很棒的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: There was this piece of press that our media is very sensationalist by definition.
zh: 当时有一篇新闻报道，我们的媒体在定义上是非常耸人听闻的。

speaker: Unknown
en: They will try to create a very polarized view about the world where there actually isn't one.
zh: 他们会试图制造一个非常两极分化的世界观，而实际上并不存在两极分化。

speaker: Unknown
en: And most things in my life, things are not as extreme as you hear of the headlines of the media.
zh: 在我生活中的大多数事情里，事情并不像你在媒体头条里听到的那么极端。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's somewhere in the middle.
zh: 它是介于两者之间的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there was one time that there was this article that ran, and it was about giving me an unnecessary amount of credit, and frankly, not giving Chuck as much credit about something that he has actually done.
zh: 有一次有一篇文章发表了，它是关于给我过多的荣誉，坦率地说，没有给 Chuck 足够的荣誉，关于他实际上做过的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of the movements that we've had internally wouldn't have happened if he had not hired me and given me agency to go do the things that I needed to get done.
zh: 如果他没有雇佣我并给我智能体去做我需要完成的事情，我们内部的许多举措就不会发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he was very much completely in sync with me on what needed to happen.
zh: 他在需要发生什么事情上与我完全同步。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, when I saw this article, I had no idea who the report, I reached out, I'm like, "Hey, I just want to let you know, this was not me saying it's someone."
zh: 所以，当我看到这篇文章时，我不知道记者是谁，我联系了对方，我说，“嘿，我只是想让你知道，这不是我说的，这是某人。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And she said, "Don't worry about it, man."
zh: 她说，“别担心，伙计。”

speaker: Unknown
en: What I've learned in life is, if you don't care about who gets the credit, you just go a lot farther in life."
zh: 我在生活中学到的是，如果你不在乎谁得到功劳，你在生活中会走得远得多。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's so profound in so many ways that he's just way too confident to let anything.
zh: 这在很多时候都是如此深刻，他只是太自信了，以至于不会让任何事情……

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, the thing I've learned from Chuck is the importance of confidence and the importance of knowing what you're good at and where you're not good at.
zh: 所以，我从 Chuck 那里学到的是自信的重要性，以及知道你擅长什么和不擅长什么的重要性。

speaker: Unknown
en: And where you're not good, you're going to assemble a team of people around you.
zh: 在你不擅长的地方，你要在周围组建一个团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's just masterful at that, and it happens-
zh: 他在这方面非常娴熟，这发生在——

speaker: Unknown
en: And by the way, he's the CEO of Cisco, in case people-
zh: 顺便说一下，他是 Cisco 的 CEO，以防人们不知道——

speaker: Unknown
en: That's right.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's the CEO of Cisco.
zh: 他是 Cisco 的 CEO。

speaker: Unknown
en: He is the chair of the business round table.
zh: 他是商业圆桌会议的主席。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's very dear friend of mine.
zh: 他是我非常亲爱的朋友。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I feel like there's a lot to learn from that kind of mental model and mindset.
zh: 我觉得有很多东西可以从这种心智模型和心态中学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I've been lucky enough, Lenny, that, and this is just dumb luck.
zh: 我很幸运，Lenny，这纯粹是愚蠢的运气。

speaker: Unknown
en: The people that I've worked with and for are all very, very close to me, and I just don't let them go from my life.
zh: 我与之共事和为之工作的人都非常非常亲近，我只是不让他们从我的生活中离开。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, one of the things, for example, is I worked with Aaron and then when I was leaving, it was very emotional, but I wanted to do something different.
zh: 所以，其中一件事是，例如，我和 Aaron 一起工作，然后当我离开时，非常情绪化，但我想做点不同的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: But we committed to each other that we are going to have dinner every six weeks.
zh: 但我们彼此承诺，我们要每六周吃一次晚餐。

speaker: Unknown
en: And Aaron, and there's another co-founder, Jeff Kweiser, and I, three of us.
zh: Aaron，还有另一位联合创始人 Jeff Kweiser，还有我，我们三个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every six weeks in Palo Alto we have dinner.
zh: 每六周我们在 Palo Alto 吃晚餐。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's one of the most special things that I still do, and it's a tradition now, it's been going on for six years, and I love it.
zh: 这是我仍然做的最特别的事情之一，这现在是一个传统，已经持续了六年，我很喜欢它。

speaker: Unknown
en: You look at someone like Chuck, I start with my day with talking to him in the morning, we text each other.
zh: 你看看像 Chuck 这样的人，我的一天从早上和他说话开始，我们给对方发短信。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then I end the day talking to them in the evening.
zh: 然后我在晚上和他交谈结束这一天。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we probably touch base at least four or five times a day.
zh: 我们每天至少联系四五次。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're not long conversations at all points in time, but we're constantly in contact with each other.
zh: 这些并不是在任何时候都很长的对话，但我们不断地与对方保持联系。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I feel like that only happens when you've established enough trust.
zh: 我觉得只有当你建立了足够的信任时，这种情况才会发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: My first post when I moved to California is this guy named Rick Devenuti and then another guy named Jeremy Burton.
zh: 我搬到加州后的第一个职位是和一个叫 Rick Devenuti 的家伙，还有另一个叫 Jeremy Burton 的家伙。

speaker: Unknown
en: Rick Devenuti is still my coach.
zh: Rick Devenuti 仍然是我的教练。

speaker: Unknown
en: I see him every two weeks.
zh: 我每两周见他一次。

speaker: Unknown
en: Jeremy is someone that's a very dear friend of mine, and we're neighbors and be moved and bought a place next to his just so that we could be close to him.
zh: Jeremy 是我非常亲爱的朋友，我们是邻居，并且搬家后买了他隔壁的地方，就是为了能离他近一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And these are special people in your life that have enriched your life in very different ways that I think you just have to make sure that you treasure.
zh: 这些是你生活中特别的人，他们以非常不同的方式丰富了你的生活，我认为你必须确保你要珍惜他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: Today's episode is brought to you by Samsara.
zh: 今天的节目由 Samsara 赞助。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you listen to this podcast, you know that we spend a lot of time talking about building things that sit on a screen, onboarding funnels, mobile apps and checkout flows.
zh: 如果你收听这个播客，你知道我们花了很多时间谈论构建那些在屏幕上的东西，入门流程，移动应用程序和结账流程。

speaker: Unknown
en: Samsara is building products for the physical world, first responders racing to emergencies, truck drivers carrying critical supplies, construction workers building our cities and data centers.
zh: Samsara 正在为物理世界构建产品，第一响应者飞奔去紧急情况，卡车司机运送关键物资，建筑工人建造我们的城市和数据中心。

speaker: Unknown
en: These are people who put everything on the line every single day, and Samsara's technology protects them.
zh: 这些人每天都会倾尽全力，Samsara 的技术保护着他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: Samsara is solving complex problems at the intersection of hardware, software, and Edge AI, and their AI doesn't just detect events, it reasons about the intent and answers questions like, "Did that truck driver break abruptly because they were distracted, or was that a heroic act?"
zh: Samsara 正在解决硬件、软件和 Edge AI 交叉领域的复杂问题，他们的 AI 不仅检测事件，还会对意图进行推理，并回答诸如「那名卡车司机急刹车是因为他分心了，还是那是一次英勇的举动？」这样的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you want to ground LLMs in messy real-world telemetry or solve Edge AI constraints at a planetary scale, Samsara wants to talk to you.
zh: 如果你想把 LLM 落地于混乱的现实世界遥测数据，或在全球范围内解决 Edge AI 的限制，Samsara 想和你谈谈。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you like playing with enormous datasets, moving fast and working in small teams, come help build the technology that makes the physical world safer and more efficient.
zh: 如果你喜欢玩转海量数据集、快速行动并在小团队中工作，来帮助构建让物理世界更安全、更高效的技术吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Visit samsara.com/lenny to learn more.
zh: 访问 samsara.com/lenny 了解更多信息。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's S-A-M-S-A-R-A.com/Lenny.
zh: 那是 S-A-M-S-A-R-A.com/Lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, you're currently CPO at Cisco.
zh: 那么，你目前是 Cisco 的 CPO。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the team under you, IS it 25,000 people?
zh: 我想你领导的团队，是 25,000 人吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that the right number?
zh: 这个数字对吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: We have about 30,000, but-
zh: 我们大约有 30,000 人，但是——

speaker: Unknown
en: 30,000 people.

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's something that you wish you'd known before taking on this role?
zh: 有什么你希望在承担这个角色之前就知道的事情？

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if it was ... I mean, I instinctively kind of knew it, but it was very, very accentuated at Cisco, because when people say, "Oh, is scale hard?"
zh: 我不知道是不是……我的意思是，我本能地有点知道这一点，但这在 Cisco 被非常、非常地凸显出来，因为当人们说，「噢，规模很难吗？」

speaker: Unknown
en: And my perspective has always been that the absence of scale is way harder than scale.
zh: 而我的观点一直是，缺乏规模比拥有规模要难得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: What do I mean by that?
zh: 我是什么意思？

speaker: Unknown
en: If I have a startup with three people and we need to prosecute another idea, and that idea requires five people working on it, I have to go raise money, or I have to pivot my entire business.
zh: 如果我有一个 3 人的初创公司，我们需要去落实另一个想法，而这个想法需要 5 个人来从事它，我就必须去筹集资金，或者我必须转型我的整个业务。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you have 30,000 people and you have an idea that requires five people, you just figure out a way that you allocate the dollars internally and say, "Let's go prosecute this idea."
zh: 如果你有 30,000 人，而你有一个需要 5 个人的想法，你只需要想办法在内部分配资金，并说，『让我们去落实这个想法。』

speaker: Unknown
en: So, in my mind, I always felt like absence of scale was way harder than the presence of scale.
zh: 所以，在我心中，我总是觉得缺乏规模比拥有规模要难得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: And operating within scale seemed like it was like, yeah, you have more opportunity to do it.
zh: 在规模之内运作似乎就像是，是的，你有更多的机会去做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I found over the years, not just at Cisco, but even when I ... Because I ran a small startup in Chicago for 17 years before I moved over to the Valley, what I found in the large companies is, the communication framework and the lossiness of communication, the telephone game, so to say, has a profoundly negative effect if you're not intentional about it and if you're not careful of it.
zh: 这些年来我发现，不仅仅是在 Cisco，甚至即使当我……因为在搬到 Valley 之前我在芝加哥经营了一家小型初创公司 17 年，我在大公司中发现的是，沟通框架和沟通的损耗，也就是所谓的传话游戏，如果你不是刻意为之，如果你不小心的话，会产生极其负面的影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there was this board member that we had, there's a couple board members, our lead director, Michael Capellas is amazing.
zh: 我们有过那位董事会成员，有几位董事会成员，我们的首席董事 Michael Capellas 很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's this other board member, Kevin is amazing, and then there's this one board member, Wes Bush, who we recently rolled off, but he used to be on our board.
zh: 还有另一位董事会成员，Kevin 很棒，然后还有这一位董事会成员，Wes Bush，他最近卸任了，但他曾经在我们的董事会里。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when I got this job, he pulled me aside, said, "Jeetu, I'm going to tell you something.
zh: 当我得到这份工作时，他把我拉到一边，说，「Jeetu，我要告诉你一件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to give you some advice, and take it or leave it, but I think it's going to be important for you to keep it in mind."
zh: 我要给你一些建议，听不听由你，但我认为你记住这一点很重要。」

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "What's that?"
zh: 我说，「是什么？」

speaker: Unknown
en: And he goes, "Whatever you do, don't think about your story of the company as a marketing exercise.
zh: 然后他说，「无论你做什么，不要把你的公司故事仅仅当作一种营销练习。

speaker: Unknown
en: Think about it as the most intrinsic foundational exercise of the company.
zh: 把它视为公司最内在的基础性练习。

speaker: Unknown
en: And always be the custodian of the message.
zh: 并且要始终做信息的守护者。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't delegate that to someone else to give.
zh: 不要把这件事委派给别人去传达。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because if you have 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 layers between you and the person who's actually doing the job in the front line, what you don't want to do is play the telephone game and assume that people will just cascade it when you go to your team and then say, 'Okay, that team will cascade to the next team, cascade to the next team, cascade to the next team.'
zh: 因为如果在你和实际在一线工作的人之间隔着 3、4、5、6、7 层，你不想做的就是玩传话游戏，并假设人们只会层层传递，当你去找你的团队，然后说，『好的，那个团队会传给下一个团队，传给下一个团队，传给下一个团队。』

speaker: Unknown
en: Every one of them will add a flavor with well-intentioned, and then by the time it gets to the end, people won't know what it is.
zh: 他们每一个人都会出于好意而添加一点自己的理解，然后当它传到最后时，人们就不会知道它是什么了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So always own telling the story."
zh: 所以要始终掌握讲故事的主动权。」

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm like, "That seems like it's a lot because we have a very broad portfolio.
zh: 我就说，「这似乎工作量很大，因为我们拥有非常广泛的产品组合。

speaker: Unknown
en: We do all of these events.
zh: 我们要做所有这些活动。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, I'm going to have to stand on stage for 90 minutes and just talk about it."
zh: 就好像，我将不得不在台上站 90 分钟光谈论它。」

speaker: Unknown
en: He's like, "Please do that.
zh: 他说，「请务必那样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: Make sure you don't."
zh: 确保你不要不那样做。」

speaker: Unknown
en: And initially, the hidden benefit that came out of it that I did not realize is it massively, Lenny, simplified our business.
zh: 最初，从中产生的一个我没有意识到的隐性好处是，它极大地，Lenny，简化了我们的业务。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you know why?
zh: 你知道为什么吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because we have such a broad business with so many different industries, it's impossible for someone to be a deep expert in every single one of them across the board.
zh: 因为我们拥有如此广泛的业务，涉及如此多不同的行业，任何人都不可能在所有行业中都成为深度的专家。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's just way too much surface area.
zh: 表面积实在太大了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the things that we want to convey to the market that the market should take away from us, if that story is not something that I understand well enough to be able to convey it, how do I first expect 20,000 of my sellers to be able to go tell it to the market?
zh: 但是，我们要传达给市场、希望市场从我们这里带走的东西，如果那个故事不是我所理解得足够透彻以至于能够传达的，我首先怎么能期望我的 20,000 名销售人员中有人能够去把它讲给市场听？

speaker: Unknown
en: And how do I expect my customers to be able to digest that story?
zh: 我又怎么能期望我的客户能够消化那个故事？

speaker: Unknown
en: There's zero chance that would happen.
zh: 那是不可能发生的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, that was my big takeaway from this, which is don't delegate the storytelling.
zh: 所以，那是我的最大收获，就是不要委派讲故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the storytelling is not a marketing exercise after you built the product.
zh: 讲故事不是你在构建产品之后才做的营销练习。

speaker: Unknown
en: The story is why you build the product to make the story come real.
zh: 故事是你构建产品的原因，是为了让故事成真。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, make sure that the story is there first, and then that story has evidence and proof based on the products that you're building.
zh: 所以，确保故事先在那里，然后那个故事有你正在构建的产品作为证据和证明。

speaker: Unknown
en: I had a conversation with Matt MacInnis, who's COO now CPO at Rippling, and hit a similar piece of advice, which I think is also, it's like adjacent advice, which is "The intensity of an idea or a plan drops at every level that it goes from CO to the next layer and layer.
zh: 我和 Matt MacInnis 进行了一次谈话，他现在是 Rippling 的 COO，也是 CPO，我们谈到了类似的建议，我觉得这也是，它是相邻的建议，就是「一个想法或计划的强度，当它从 CO 一层层传到下一层时，都会下降。」

speaker: Unknown
en: And your job as a leader is to maintain that intensity, not to buffer it from the employees, but to maintain exactly the same intensity.""
zh: 而你作为领导者的工作，就是维持那种强度，不是为了把它从员工那里缓冲隔离，而是为了维持完全相同的强度。」

speaker: Unknown
en: And it feels like that's in addition to also just keep the story the same.
zh: 感觉这就像是，除了保持故事一致性之外。

speaker: Unknown
en: Like don't filter it, don't change it.
zh: 就像不要过滤它，不要改变它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Although your advice is even different, just like you actually go to the team working on it and tell the story yourself.
zh: 虽然你的建议甚至更不同，就像你实际上会去从事这项工作的团队那里，亲自讲述这个故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't even let-
zh: 甚至不要让——

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to make sure that they hear it from me directly so that there's no lossiness.
zh: 我想确保他们直接从我这里听到，这样就没有任何损耗。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have this concept in networking called packet loss.
zh: 我们在网络中有一个概念叫作丢包。

speaker: Unknown
en: When you actually send packets over a wire and you have a loss of packet, then actually there's loss of data.
zh: 当你通过线路发送数据包时发生了丢包，那么实际上就会丢失数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't want to have packet loss in your storytelling from you to the person on the front line because-
zh: 你不希望在讲故事时，从你到一线员工之间出现丢包，因为——

speaker: Unknown
en: The direct ethernet Cat 5 connection.
zh: 那是直连的 Cat 5 以太网连接。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is just a direct connection and there's no packet loss of this one.
zh: 这是一种直接连接，这一次没有丢包。

speaker: Unknown
en: You got to make sure it gets to the intended audience.
zh: 你必须确保它传达给了目标受众。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think the reason for that is, as companies get large, they can lose touch with the front lines.
zh: 我认为原因在于，随着公司变大，它们可能会与前线失去联系。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone gets really good with the math of the business, but they don't really always preserve the soul of the business.
zh: 每个人都擅长业务的数学层面，但他们并不总能保留业务的灵魂。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's a lossiness that happens, because if you have seven, eight layers between you and the front line, even the message that's coming back to you from them is actually getting lossy.
zh: 这里会发生损耗，因为如果你和前线之间隔着七八层，即使是传回给你的信息实际上也在变得有损耗。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so what you have to do is just preserve ... And I think what was said earlier about the intensity is the same way, which is you got to preserve the intensity.
zh: 所以你必须做的就是保留…… 我认为刚才提到的关于强度的观点也是同样的道理，就是你必须保留强度。

speaker: Unknown
en: You got to preserve the sanctity of the message, and you got to preserve the clarity of the message so that everyone is clear on the direction we're going down.
zh: 你必须保留信息的神圣性，你必须保留信息的清晰度，以便每个人都清楚我们要走的方向。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you can stay clear and stay motivated about that direction and make sure that everyone's on the same page and what needs to be done to execute, you will have success.
zh: 如果你能保持清晰，并对那个方向保持动力，并确保每个人都在同一频道上，以及清楚执行需要做什么，你就会获得成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: If not, you'll actually have guaranteed failure.
zh: 否则，你实际上将保证失败。

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you actually operationalize this without just being overloaded with work and constantly having to meet with every team and remind them of the story?
zh: 你实际上如何落实这一点，而不仅仅是工作量过载，还得不断地与每个团队开会提醒他们这个故事？

speaker: Unknown
en: The first thing I feel is, you have to have very clear thinking because the clarity of thought is what brings clarity of communication.
zh: 我首先感觉到的是，你必须有非常清晰的思考，因为思维的清晰带来了沟通的清晰。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, you have to spend the time with your team in sweating the details on what it is that you want to do and why you want to do it.
zh: 所以，你必须花时间和你的团队一起，在你想做什么以及为什么想做这些事情上打磨细节。

speaker: Unknown
en: The context of why is so lost, and constantly reminding people why it's important and having the least amount of asymmetry between the topmost layer in the organization and the bottommost layer is super.
zh: 为什么的背景经常丢失，不断提醒人们为什么它很重要，并在组织最顶层和最底层之间保持最小的不对称性，是非常重要的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, by the way, I'm a Section 16 officer.
zh: 顺便说一句，我是 Section 16 高管。

speaker: Unknown
en: There are certain things that, for example, you're in a quiet period you can't go talk about to someone else during that time period, but that's not allowed.
zh: 有些事情，比如当你处于静默期时，你不能在那段时间和别人谈论这些，但那是不允许的。

speaker: Unknown
en: However, the most amount of context that you can provide them in the way that you can because you're allowed to, the better off you are.
zh: 然而，只要是被允许的，你能以某种方式给他们提供尽可能多的背景信息，你的境况就越好。

speaker: Unknown
en: And always treat people like adults.
zh: 并且永远把人当作成年人对待。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I've found is, oftentimes when you go into corporate environments, like people start becoming very sterile in the facts that they provide.
zh: 我发现的是，通常当你进入企业环境时，人们开始在他们提供的事实中变得非常 sterile。

speaker: Unknown
en: And sometimes it's okay to just say, "Hey, we screwed up here.
zh: 有时候没关系，直接说，「嘿，我们在这里搞砸了。

speaker: Unknown
en: This was really bad."
zh: 这真的很糟糕。」

speaker: Unknown
en: That's not meant to ... One of the things that I found to be very counterintuitive, because every management book that you read will tell you otherwise.
zh: 这并不是要…… 我发现非常违反直觉的一件事，因为你读到的每一本管理书都会告诉你相反的情况。

speaker: Unknown
en: What do they say?
zh: 他们说什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Praise in public and criticize in private.
zh: 公开表扬，私下批评。

speaker: Unknown
en: I fundamentally disagree with that notion.
zh: 我根本不同意那个观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think what you have to do is establish enough trust among the team so that you are comfortable critiquing and debating in public.
zh: 我认为你必须做的是在团队中建立足够的信任，这样你对在公开场合进行批评和辩论会感到舒适。

speaker: Unknown
en: But when you're in private, take that moment to build the trust.
zh: 但当你私下时，要利用那个时刻去建立信任。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because if you build that trust and you tell them that you've got their back and you create a level of safety there in public, you don't want to be in a mode of posturing.
zh: 因为如果你建立了那种信任，你告诉他们你会支持他们，并且你在公开场合那里创造了一种安全感，你不想处于一种摆姿态的模式。

speaker: Unknown
en: You want to be in a mode of problem-solving.
zh: 你想处于一种解决问题的模式。

speaker: Unknown
en: When you're just giving people perfunctory compliments all the time, and everything's just hunky-dory.
zh: 当你只是一直给人们表面的恭维，一切都很 hunky-dory。

speaker: Unknown
en: Rose-colored glass is great.
zh: 玫瑰色的眼镜很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: All your dashboards look green, but you're growing the business at 1.5%.
zh: 你所有的仪表盘看起来都是绿色的，但你的业务只增长 1.5%。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's an asymmetry there, something's broken.
zh: 那里有一个不对称，有些东西坏了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, what do we need to do over here?
zh: 就像，我们需要在这里做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so what I tend to do is use the exact opposite approach.
zh: 所以我倾向于使用完全相反的方法。

speaker: Unknown
en: I tend to be very, very direct in public, be respectful, but be direct in public.
zh: 我倾向于在公开场合非常、非常直接，要尊重，但在公开场合要直接。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is not working.
zh: 这行不通。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let me tell you why it's not working.
zh: 让我告诉你为什么它行不通。

speaker: Unknown
en: We got to face the facts.
zh: 我们必须面对事实。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then be very, very clear with people that you got their back in private.
zh: 然后非常、非常清楚地告诉人们，你在私下会支持他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: And don't be stingy with words on that front, because I feel like there are times when people are very stingy with words with people in private.
zh: 在这方面不要吝惜言辞，因为我觉得有时候人们对私底下的人很吝惜言辞。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can't be stingy with words over there.
zh: 在那边你不能吝惜言辞。

speaker: Unknown
en: And don't be stingy with critique in public, because I think people need to make sure that, "We are solving problems together."
zh: 并且不要在公开场合吝惜批评，因为我认为人们需要确保，“我们要一起解决问题。”

speaker: Unknown
