en: And if we don't know the play that we're executing, if we don't know the things that we're going to need to do, then I am not really certain.
zh: 如果我们不知道我们正在执行的战术，如果我们不知道我们需要做的事情，那么我就不是很确定。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're making collective progress, and I think it's not going to be fulfilling to either you or the recipient at some point, and those compliments will feel hollow because you didn't mean them.
zh: 如果你正在取得集体进步，我认为这对你或接收者在某个时刻都不会是令人满足的，那些赞美会显得空洞，因为你并不是真心实意的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because you were trying to put it in between.
zh: 因为你是试图把它夹在中间。

speaker: Unknown
en: Like Ben Horowitz says, and hard things about hard things that you have a shit sandwich.
zh: 就像 Ben Horowitz 说的，以及关于《The Hard Thing About Hard Things》里的那个糟糕的三明治。

speaker: Unknown
en: You say something really nice to someone, then you say something that's not really nice, and then you put no, just treat people like adults.
zh: 你先对某人说了一些真正好听的话，然后你说了一些不太好的话，然后你把，不，就把人当成年人对待。

speaker: Unknown
en: Tell them the facts, watch your tone.
zh: 告诉他们事实，注意你的语气。

speaker: Unknown
en: I still have to work on that.
zh: 我仍然需要在这方面努力。

speaker: Unknown
en: There are times when I get very passionate.
zh: 有时候我会变得非常充满激情。

speaker: Unknown
en: People think like, but watch your tone and make sure that you debate.
zh: 人们觉得像，但是注意你的语气并确保你在辩论。

speaker: Unknown
en: Conflict is a necessary condition of business, but the only way that you can have productive conflict is if you've established trust.
zh: 冲突是业务的必要条件，但你能富有成效地冲突的唯一途径是你已经建立了信任。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the only way that you can establish trust is by making sure that you spend the time to establish the trust.
zh: 而你能建立信任的唯一途径是确保你花时间去建立信任。

speaker: Unknown
en: So spend the time to establish the trust, but then focus on the best idea, winning and actually having the debate.
zh: 所以花时间去建立信任，然后专注于最好的想法，获胜并真正进行辩论。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there maybe one more lesson that you've learned from this?
zh: 也许还有一个你从中学到的教训？

speaker: Unknown
en: Or I guess it's something you wish you'd known before getting into this role?
zh: 或者我猜这是你在进入这个角色之前希望知道的？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there anything else that comes to mind?
zh: 还有什么其他的想到的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I was an apps guy.
zh: 我是一个应用层的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I operated in the apps layer.
zh: 我在应用层运作。

speaker: Unknown
en: I worked at Box.
zh: 我在 Box 工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And even when I was at EMC, I was building apps that you built for the end user.
zh: 即使我在 EMC 的时候，我在构建为最终用户构建的应用。

speaker: Unknown
en: Infrastructure is a different game.
zh: 基础设施是一个不同的游戏。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the thing that I learned about infrastructure is, you don't always get the glory, but you always get the blame.
zh: 我学到的关于基础设施的一点是，你并不总是能得到荣耀，但你总是会得到指责。

speaker: Unknown
en: Perfect.
zh: 完美。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you have to be comfortable with the fact that you are working in a way that other people get the glory.
zh: 你必须对这一事实感到舒适，即你以其他人获得荣耀的方式工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: Great infrastructure companies, the application companies get the glory when they're running on that infrastructure.
zh: 伟大的基础设施公司，当应用公司在其基础设施上运行时，应用公司获得荣耀。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, you have to be hardwired in infrastructure to orient on your ecosystem success, not just your own success.
zh: 所以，你必须在基础设施中以此为固有设定，即关注你的生态系统的成功，而不仅仅是你自己的成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that is probably one of the lessons that I learned at Cisco in a very stark way, which I didn't fully appreciate it until I got into the details of the infrastructure going, "Wow, if this thing doesn't work," we were ... Every single time, our infrastructure doesn't work.
zh: 那可能是我在 Cisco 学到的教训之一，以一种非常鲜明的方式，直到我深入基础设施的细节时我才完全 appreciate 它，意识到，“哇，如果这东西不工作，”我们是……每一次，我们的基础设施都不工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: This morning I was with a medical institution, I was with a healthcare company this morning, and they were telling me they were very complimentary.
zh: 今天早上我和一家医疗机构在一起，今天早上我和一家医疗保健公司在一起，他们非常赞赏。

speaker: Unknown
en: They were thanking us on the partnership.
zh: 他们感谢我们的合作关系。

speaker: Unknown
en: I asked them, "Why are you doubling down with us?"
zh: 我问他们，“为什么要加倍投入与我们合作？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And they're like, "Because when the infrastructure doesn't work, people die.
zh: 他们说，“因为当基础设施不工作时，人会死。

speaker: Unknown
en: Someone doesn't get dialysis, someone doesn't get a surgery done, and we need to make sure that we're working with someone with the infrastructure is working."
zh: 有人没法做透析，有人没法做手术，我们需要确保我们正在与基础设施工作正常的人合作。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, I feel like at that point in time, you can't be navel-gazing too much about, "Look how cool you are, because you did something."
zh: 所以，我觉得在那个时候，你不能过分地自我陶醉于，“看你是多么酷，因为你做了一些事情。”

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to just make sure that you're really immediately shifting your focus to, what does the customer do and what does the ecosystem do with your infrastructure so that the outcome is achieved?
zh: 你必须确保你真正立即将你的注意力转移到，客户做什么以及生态系统用你的基础设施做什么，以便实现结果？

speaker: Unknown
en: And you have to get very outcome oriented.
zh: 你必须变得非常以结果为导向。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I feel like that was something that I always intellectually knew, but I didn't fully realize it until I came here on how important of a mindset shift that is.
zh: 我觉得那是我一直理智上知道的事情，但直到我来到这里才完全意识到它，这是一个多么重要的思维转变。

speaker: Unknown
en: You are not talking about yourself, you're talking about the system just working.
zh: 你不是在谈论你自己，你是在谈论系统正常工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: No one will come and tell you, "Hey, Jeetu, thank you so much.
zh: 没有人会来告诉你，“嘿，Jeetu，非常感谢。

speaker: Unknown
en: My network worked today."
zh: 我的网络今天工作了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But the moment it doesn't work, they're going to call you and say, "You know what?
zh: 但当它不工作的那一刻，他们会打电话给你说，“你知道吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: My network's not working, and my people can't work and patients are dying in the hospital."
zh: 我的网络不工作了，我的人没法工作，病人在医院里快死了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I think you just have to be comfortable with that.
zh: 我认为你必须对那个感到舒适。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's so interesting how this lesson connects so directly to the lesson you learned from Chuck, the CEO of Cisco, which is, "Don't expect the praise and the credit.
zh: 这一课如何直接联系到你从 Cisco 的 CEO Chuck 那里学到的教训，这太有趣了，那就是，“不要期待赞扬和功劳。

speaker: Unknown
en: You need to be comfortable with other people getting credit for your work."
zh: 你需要对其他人因为你的工作而获得功劳感到舒适。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That's right.
zh: 没错。

speaker: Unknown
en: By the way, it's not surprising given that he spent, I don't know, 26, 28 years over here, you know why that he's conditioned with the fact that he's focused on other people succeeding from it.
zh: 顺便说一下，这并不令人惊讶，考虑到他在这里花了，我不知道，26，28 年，你知道为什么他被以这一事实为条件，即他专注于其他人从中成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: From your work.
zh: 从你的工作中。

speaker: Unknown
en: It feels like there's so many metaphors corollaries to networking as a way to think about leadership and living life.
zh: 感觉好像有很多比喻和类比到网络作为一种思考领导力和生活的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: It really is.
zh: 确实是这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh man, I bet you guys have all kinds of examples.
zh: 天哪，我敢打赌你们有各种各样的例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a good exercise to actually go through and create the corollary of parallelism between life and networks.
zh: 这是一个很好的练习，实际上去经历并创造生活和网络之间平行关系的推论。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm thinking about just how many friends, like Dunbar's number, how many notes can you have in a network before it starts to slow down?
zh: 我在想有多少朋友，就像邓巴数一样，在一个网络中你能有多少个节点，在它开始变慢之前？

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe 150.
zh: 也许 150 个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, man.
zh: 天哪。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Anyway, I like that your mind's spinning.
zh: 无论如何，我喜欢你的思维在飞速运转。

speaker: Jeetu Patel
en: I'm thinking how many can you have? I think more than 150, for sure.
zh: 我在想你能有多少个？我认为肯定超过 150 个。

speaker: Unknown
en: I also was thinking about Intel.
zh: 我也在想英特尔。

speaker: Unknown
en: The whole Intel Inside move was such a clever way to break through that where no one would know Intel and so they're just like, slap a sticker of Intel Inside.
zh: 整个 Intel Inside 的举措是突破这一点的非常聪明的方式，在那之前没人知道英特尔，所以他们就像，啪地贴一张 Intel Inside 的贴纸。

speaker: Unknown
en: And by the way, they are.
zh: 顺便说一句，他们确实是。

speaker: Unknown
en: Lipu is a very dear friend.
zh: Lipu 是一个非常亲爱的朋友。

speaker: Unknown
en: Pat Gelsinger used to be my mentor at EMC.
zh: Pat Gelsinger 曾经是我在 EMC 的导师。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so both those people that have had such a profound contribution to that industry in general.
zh: 所以这两个人都对那个行业做出了如此深远的贡献。

speaker: Unknown
en: When you start thinking about them, they're very, very much on that mode.
zh: 当你开始想到他们时，他们非常、非常处于那种模式。

speaker: Unknown
en: I could see how you pull together this insane collection of humans, just feels like you're just friends with everybody.
zh: 我能看出你是如何聚集这群疯狂的人类的，感觉就像你和每个人都是朋友。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like it's, life's too short not to be, and I'm only friends with people that I feel are good human beings.
zh: 我觉得是这样的，人生苦短不得不这样，而且我只和我觉得是好人的人交朋友。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I try not to do is I try to minimize my time no matter how successful they are with people whose energy I don't vibe with, because I think life's too short.
zh: 我尽量不做的是，尽量减少我和气场不合的人在一起的时间，无论他们多么成功，因为我认为人生苦短。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in my mind, one of the most off-putting things is, look, all of us have a healthy ego.
zh: 在我脑海里，最令人反感的事情之一是，看，我们都有一个健康的自我。

speaker: Unknown
en: There are times when ego gets manifested with insecurity and you have to make sure that you're at least self-aware enough to know when your ego is starting to take over your behavior in a way that's counterproductive.
zh: 有时候，自我会表现出不安全感，你必须确保自己至少有足够的自我意识，知道你的自我何时开始以适得其反的方式接管你的行为。

speaker: Unknown
en: And all of those things are super important.
zh: 所有这些事情都非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: But what I think is extremely important is that you ... Life is just fun to live when you love the people you are around.
zh: 但我认为极其重要的是，你……当你爱你周围的人时，生活就是充满乐趣的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Can I digress for a second in this one story that ... I'll tell you the story that was ... So my mother was, she passed away two and a half years ago, but she was extremely sick in the hospital for eight weeks before she passed away.
zh: 我能岔开一下讲个故事吗……我会告诉你那个故事……所以我母亲在，她两年半前去世了，但在她去世前她在医院病了八周。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I was very close to my mom.
zh: 我和我的妈妈非常亲近。

speaker: Unknown
en: She was my everything and we were only child.
zh: 她是我的一切，而且我们是独生子。

speaker: Unknown
en: I grew up with a rough childhood.
zh: 我是在艰难的童年中长大的。

speaker: Unknown
en: My dad was a high-stakes kind of conn man, like Bernie Madoff.
zh: 我爸爸是一个高风险的骗术高手，像 Bernie Madoff 那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: I didn't want to be any part of that.
zh: 我不想参与那里面的任何事。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I had actually left India, come over here, hadn't seen him.
zh: 所以我实际上离开了印度，来到了这里，没再见过他。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so he was very abusive to my mom.
zh: 所以他对我妈妈非常虐待。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there was a bunch of that that had happened.
zh: 所以发生了很多那样的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, we had had a very difficult early childhood life for me, and her and I had bonded during that time at a very deep level.
zh: 所以，我们对我来说度过了一个非常艰难的早期童年生活，她和我在这段时间里在非常深的层面上建立起了纽带。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so when she came to America, she always wanted to have her own place, but she lived very close by and she was very dependent on me emotionally in every way.
zh: 所以当她来到美国时，她总是想拥有自己的地方，但她住得很近，她在情感上在各方面都非常依赖我。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, I had almost become a parent to her.
zh: 所以，我几乎成了她的家长。

speaker: Unknown
en: And at the last eight weeks things flipped and she became a parent again.
zh: 在最后八周情况反过来了，她又再次成为了家长。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we were getting to the point where she was ending her journey.
zh: 所以我们到了她即将结束旅程的阶段。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I was sitting one in the morning at the bedside by her in the hospital.
zh: 一天凌晨一点我坐在医院的她床边。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was living in the hospital at the time.
zh: 那时我住在医院里。

speaker: Unknown
en: And she was sleeping and I was just crying profusely.
zh: 她在睡觉，我只是在痛哭流涕。

speaker: Unknown
en: And she wakes up, and she knows why I'm crying, because she's going to be gone soon.
zh: 她醒来了，她知道我为什么哭，因为她很快就要离开了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And she looks at me, Lenny, and she's like all perplexed and she's like, "I had no idea that you loved me so much."
zh: 她看着我，叫着 Lenny，她看起来很困惑，她说：“我不知道你这么爱我。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, by the way, this is the most abnormal thing for me to hear, because I'm like, "What are you talking about mom?
zh: 顺便说一句，这对我来说是最不正常的事情，因为我是说，“你在说什么呀妈妈？

speaker: Unknown
en: Like you're one of the most important people in my life."
zh: 你是我生命中最重要的人之一。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And everything that I did was to make sure that my mom was okay.
zh: 我所做的一切都是为了确保我的妈妈没事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why did it feel that way to her?
zh: 为什么她会感觉到那样？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because she didn't know how I was thinking.
zh: 因为她不知道我是怎么想的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that kind of notion of people don't know what's going on in your mind is so important that my biggest lesson from that was, "Don't be stingy with words."
zh: 那种人们不知道你心里在想什么的概念太重要了，我从中得到的一个最大的教训是，“不要吝啬语言。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Because even my mother that knows me inside and out didn't know how much I loved her, that there's no chance that people in the business world are going to know how you feel if you're not explicit with them.
zh: 因为即使是那个里里外外了解我的母亲都不知道我有多爱她，那么如果你在商业世界中不明确表达，那些人就没有机会知道你的感觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, I'm actually very clear with people, when I find them and when I find them rewarding, I let them know how much they mean, because I genuinely find a lot of energy coming out of that.
zh: 所以，实际上我非常清楚地对待人们，当我发现他们，并且发现他们很有价值时，我会让他们知道他们对我意义重大，因为我确实从中发现了很多能量。

speaker: Unknown
en: And B, the circle of friends just keeps getting bigger and bigger and bigger.
zh: 第二，朋友圈子一直在变得越来越大。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I found that to be a super rewarding thing in life.
zh: 我发现这是生活中非常有回报的一件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you're right, most of the people that were at the AI Summit are dear friends, and isn't that just a better way to live life?
zh: 你说得对，参加 AI 峰会的大多数人都是亲密的朋友，这难道不是一种更好的生活方式吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think we've uncovered one of the secrets of your success, which is just tell people how you feel and help them see that you appreciate them.
zh: 我认为我们发现了你成功的秘诀之一，那就是告诉人们你的感受，并让他们看到你感激他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: Make it clear that you appreciate them, that you value them, which is a lot of people don't do, they just assume they know that they like you.
zh: 明确表达你感激他们，你看重他们，这是很多人不做的，他们只是假设对方知道他们喜欢你。

speaker: Unknown
en: And don't make it fake.
zh: 不要造假。

speaker: Unknown
en: And don't make it fake.
zh: 不要造假。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't make it fake.
zh: 不要造假。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you don't love someone, don't tell them you love them.
zh: 如果你不爱某人，就不要告诉他们你爱他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the other thing that I have.
zh: 这是我具备的另一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's so interesting.
zh: 这太有趣了。

speaker: Unknown
en: We just did a little interview kind of thing with my mother-in-law meant for our son, just for him to have when he's older.
zh: 我们刚才和我的岳母做了一个小采访，是打算给我儿子的，只是为了让他长大后拥有。

speaker: Unknown
en: They just interviewed her about her story and stuff and they asked her at the end of it, "What's something you want Jude," which is his name, "to know, a lesson to learn from you?"
zh: 他们采访了她关于她的故事等等，最后问她，“你希望 Jude 知道什么，”这是他的名字，“从你这里学到的一课是什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's to just, if you love someone, tell them you love them as much as you can.
zh: 就是如果你爱某人，就尽可能多地告诉他们你爱他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, that's so true.
zh: 是的，那是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're so intentional about the way in which you do these things.
zh: 你做这些事情的方式是如此的刻意。

speaker: Unknown
en: I wish I'd done a, I should do that now.
zh: 希望我当时做了个，我现在应该做那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now I think about it, do a podcast for my daughter that's only for her when she gets older.
zh: 现在我想想，给我女儿做一个播客，只是给她长大后听的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'll send you these.
zh: 我会把这些发给你。

speaker: Unknown
en: This group, they do this.
zh: 这个群体，他们做这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think they're in the Bay Area, but it's incredible.
zh: 我觉得他们在湾区，但这太不可思议了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a whole documentary thing where they interview you, film your life for a little bit and then make a whole documentary.
zh: 这是一个完整的纪录片制作，他们会采访你，拍摄你的生活一小段时间，然后制作一整部纪录片。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh really?
zh: 哦真的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, wow.
zh: 哦，哇。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'd love that actually.
zh: 我其实会很喜欢的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, man.
zh: 噢，天哪。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're going to get a lot of business right now.
zh: 他们现在会接到很多生意。

speaker: Unknown
en: There you go.
zh: 就是那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let me end with a question around just your journey.
zh: 最后让我问一个关于你的旅程的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: So today you lead product at a 90,000 person company.
zh: 今天你领导一家 9 万人公司的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: You manage 30,000 people.
zh: 你管理 3 万人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Like you said, you grew up in India in Bombay very far outside Silicon Valley.
zh: 就像你说的，你在印度孟买长大，离硅谷很远。

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of people hearing this today are kind of in a similar boat.
zh: 今天听到的很多人处境都差不多。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're way outside of the valley, maybe don't have a lot of obvious way to break in.
zh: 他们离硅谷很远，也许没有明显的方式进入。

speaker: Unknown
en: They don't have a lot of opportunity and they see someone like you and that's their dream.
zh: 他们没有很多机会，他们看到像你这样的人，那是他们的梦想。

speaker: Unknown
en: What would your advice be to someone in that place right now?
zh: 对于现在处于那个境地的人，你有什么建议？

speaker: Unknown
en: The platform that you choose and the quality of problems that you pick to solve actually determine a lot of the path of success for you.
zh: 你选择的平台和你选择解决的问题的质量实际上决定了你成功的很大一部分路径。

speaker: Unknown
en: And typically harder problems have a higher likelihood of success.
zh: 通常来说，更难的问题有更高的成功可能性。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because the harder problems are the ones that attract better people to that problem and business as a team sport.
zh: 因为更难的问题是那些能吸引更好的人来解决这个问题，而商业是一项团队运动。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you attract people to the problems that are hard and important enough to solve, then you get the best team, and you get the best team, your odds are winning just go up exponentially.
zh: 如果你吸引人们去解决那些足够困难和重要的问题，那么你就能得到最好的团队，而你得到了最好的团队，你获胜的几率就会呈指数级上升。

speaker: Unknown
en: So most people think I'm going to go out and pick an easy enough problem to solve.
zh: 所以大多数人认为我要出去找一个足够容易解决的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's like you don't get the best-team attracted to you to start up a lemonade stand, very important job.
zh: 就像你不会为了摆个柠檬水摊而吸引到最好的团队，这是非常重要的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: But that might not be the thing that actually gets the best team to come to you.
zh: 但这可能并不是那个能让最好的团队来找你的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you actually pick a hard enough problem to solve, you'll get the best team to come.
zh: 但如果你真的选择了一个足够难的问题来解决，最好的团队就会来。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's one.
zh: 这是第一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Number two, I'd say that you can teach and learn a lot of things in life.
zh: 第二点，我想说你可以在生活中教授和学习很多东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think you can learn hunger or you can't teach hunger.
zh: 我认为你学不会饥饿，或者你教不了饥饿。

speaker: Unknown
en: So find what you're intrinsically hungry about and make sure that you try to pursue that area.
zh: 所以找到你内心渴望的东西，并确保你尝试追求那个领域。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's different from passion about something.
zh: 这与对某事的激情不同。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like in everything that you do in work, you have to just understand the formula that there's going to be 30% of the stuff that you do at work that you're just going to hate.
zh: 这就像你在工作中做的每一件事一样，你必须明白这个公式，那就是你在工作中做的事情会有 30% 是你真正讨厌的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you have to get used to things that you hate that you have to do.
zh: 你必须习惯那些你讨厌但又不得不做的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: But further, but find something that you're really hungry about that makes you want to come in to work every day, because the mission is worth the expenditure of energy that you're putting into it.
zh: 但进一步说，要找到那种你真正渴望的东西，让你每天想来到工作岗位，因为那个使命值得你投入精力去完成。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'll leave you with a story which was, I hadn't gone to India in a long time.
zh: 我要给你们讲一个故事，我很久没有去过印度了。

speaker: Unknown
en: When I left India, I didn't go back.
zh: 当我离开印度后，我就没有回去过。

speaker: Unknown
en: I left in '91 and I hadn't gone back in any kind of meaningful way until 2017 because of all the trauma in childhood.
zh: 我在 91 年离开，直到 2017 年才以某种有意义的方式回去，因为童年的所有创伤。

speaker: Unknown
en: For whatever reason, I hadn't gone back.
zh: 不管是什么原因，我一直没有回去。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I took my daughter and we went to Augra to see the Taj Mahal and we went there and there was this tour guide, his name was Raj.
zh: 但是我带着女儿去了 Augra 参观泰姬陵，我们到了那里，有一位导游，他的名字叫 Raj。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this tour guide was like he understood so much about the product that he was selling, which was the tour of the Taj Mahal.
zh: 这位导游对他所销售的产品——也就是泰姬陵的游览——非常了解。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if he was making the shit up or not, but it sounded really good and he seemed like he was kind of early on it.
zh: 我不知道他是不是在胡扯，但听起来真的很棒，而且他似乎很早就懂这些了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But when we were walking back, there's all these people and he would just start talking to them and he'd bust out in different languages.
zh: 但在我们往回走的时候，周围全是人，他就会开始跟他们说话，并用不同的语言滔滔不绝。

speaker: Unknown
en: He'd talk to someone in German, talk to someone in French, someone in Spanish, someone in Hindi, someone in.
zh: 他用德语跟人说话，用法语，用西班牙语，用印地语，用……

speaker: Unknown
en: And at some point in time in Mandarin.
zh: 还有在某个时候用普通话。

speaker: Unknown
en: And at some point in time I stopped, "Dude, how many languages do you speak?"
zh: 在某个时刻我停了下来，“伙计，你会说多少种语言？”

speaker: Unknown
en: He's like, "Oh, I speak, I don't know, 12 or 14 or some ridiculous number.
zh: 他说，“噢，我会说，我不知道，12 或 14 种，或者某个荒谬的数字。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But I try to learn a new language every year."
zh: 但我试着每年学一种新语言。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "Oh, why is that?"
zh: 我说，“噢，那是为什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And he goes, "Well, I just want to honor the people that come here and not be presumptuous that they will speak in the language that I know."
zh: 他说，“嗯，我只是想尊重来到这里的人们，不要想当然地认为他们会说我会的语言。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to speak in their language.
zh: 我想用他们的语言说话。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm thinking to myself, I was a box at the time.
zh: 我心里想，我当时是个 box。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "This guy is smarter than every person on the executive team and probably just as smart as every salesperson we have, but he's making $10 a day and all of us are enjoying this amazing life, and it's because we have access to a platform and he doesn't."
zh: 我说，“这家伙比高管团队的每个人都聪明，可能跟我们要有的每个销售人员一样聪明，但他一天赚 10 美元，而我们都在享受这美好的生活，这是因为我们有平台可以使用，而他没有。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So when people start confusing life thinking that, "Everything that I've earned is because of my amazing abilities."
zh: 所以当人们开始混淆生活，认为“我拥有的一切都是因为我惊人的能力”时

speaker: Unknown
en: I always question that, because there's a lot of luck in this thing.
zh: 我总是对此提出质疑，因为这里面有很多运气的成分。

speaker: Unknown
en: But when luck does present itself, be extremely prepared to capitalize on it.
zh: 但当运气真的来临时，要做好充分利用它的准备。

speaker: Unknown
en: And make sure that you pick the platform that can actually give you that springboard.
zh: 并确保你选择了一个真正能给你那个跳板的平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because platforms really matter.
zh: 因为平台真的很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if we, like I had the platform and benefit of America, of education, of being in tech, of having great friends and mentors, all of those things created compounding value.
zh: 如果我们，就像我拥有美国的平台和福利，拥有教育，处于科技行业，拥有伟大的朋友和导师，所有这些都创造了复合价值。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I intentionally sought out those platforms, seek out the platform, be obsessed about being extremely prepared, and don't be intellectually lazy.
zh: 但我是刻意寻找那些平台的，去寻找平台，要痴迷于做好万全准备，不要在智力上懒惰。

speaker: Unknown
en: Laziness is not a good trait.
zh: 懒惰不是一个好特质。

speaker: Unknown
en: So do the preparation that's needed.
zh: 所以要做必要的准备。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then just make sure that during that time period that you're doing, if you build a community around you of people that are vested in your success, I think it's just, life is just a more fun way to live it, rather than being the lone wolf that's going at it by themselves.
zh: 然后只要确保在那段时间里，如果你周围建立了一个由对你成功既得利益的人组成的社区，我觉得这只是，生活只是一种更有趣的生活方式，而不是做一只独来独往的狼独自奋斗。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's why I always feel like making sure that you are expressive and communicative and don't try to carry the entire world's burden on your shoulders, but actually share it with people with you, the people that you share it with actually appreciate that you're sharing it with them.
zh: 这就是为什么我总觉得要确保你善于表达和沟通，不要试图把整个世界的重担扛在你一个人的肩上，而是要把它与和你在一起的人分享，与你分享的人实际上很感激你与他们分享。

speaker: Unknown
en: And most people in the world love to help.
zh: 世界上大多数人都喜欢帮助别人。

speaker: Unknown
en: So ask for the help, but make sure that that help is not transactional.
zh: 所以寻求帮助，但要确保这种帮助不是交易性的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And don't just go to them when you need something.
zh: 不要只在需要什么东西的时候才去找他们。 [[685] 实际上试着先在足够长的时间里增加价值，不是因为某个时候你可能需要他们的什么东西，只是让自己硬连接起来去为他人增加价值。

speaker: Unknown
en: Actually try to add value first for a long enough amount of time, not because at some point you might need something from them, just hard-wire yourself into adding value to others.
zh: 其实，先试着在足够长的一段时间内增加价值，不是因为在某个时候你可能需要从他们那里得到什么，只是让自己习惯于为他人增加价值。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then eventually that value starts showing up, and life's just a better way to live life.
zh: 最终那种价值开始显现，生活只是更好的生活方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I do feel like right now it's hard for kids getting into the workforce and all of that.
zh: 我确实觉得现在对于进入职场的孩子来说很难，诸如此类。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, don't lose hope and stay persistent and have stamina, because these things go up and down.
zh: 所以，不要失去希望，保持坚持，要有耐力，因为这些东西起起伏伏。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you kind of stick with it, the people that have the most amount of persistence, it's very seldom that they don't end up winning.
zh: 但如果你能坚持下去，那些拥有最多坚持力的人，很少有不最终获胜的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Something that comes to me as you share this advice.
zh: 当你分享这些建议时，我想到了一件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Arnold Schwarzenegger's has this book that he put out, and I feel like the title of the book is the best piece of advice, and the simplest way to describe How to be Successful in Life, which is be useful.
zh: Arnold Schwarzenegger 写了那本书，我觉得这本书的标题就是最好的建议，也是描述如何在生活中取得成功的最简单方式，那就是要“有用”。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is so good.
zh: 这太好了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Jeetu, this was incredible.
zh: Jeetu，这太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there anything that we didn't cover that you wanted to share?
zh: 还有什么我们没有涵盖但你想分享的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Anything you want to leave listeners with before we get to our very exciting lightning round?
zh: 在我们进入非常令人兴奋的闪电轮之前，你有什么想留给听众的吗？

speaker: Jeetu Patel
en: I think there's a framework that I use for great companies that might be worth kind of sharing with people.
zh: 我想我有一个用于伟大公司的框架，可能值得和大家分享一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a six part framework that I have, which is in descending order of importance, and on how to build great companies.
zh: 我有一个包含六个部分的框架，它是按重要性降序排列的，关于如何建立伟大的公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is-
zh: 这是——

speaker: Unknown
en: Amazing.
zh: 太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: You get it for free, you get what you pay for it, so take it with a grain of salt.
zh: 你免费得到它，你付出多少就得到多少，所以对此持保留态度。

speaker: Unknown
en: But here's the way I think about it.
zh: 但这是我思考它的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: The most important thing is timing.
zh: 最重要的东西是时机。

speaker: Unknown
en: The six things you need in a company, if you don't have all these six, you don't win.
zh: 如果一家公司没有这六样东西，你就赢不了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But they're stack ranked in descending order of importance, but you have to have all six.
zh: 但它们是按重要性降序排列的，但你必须拥有全部六样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Number one is timing.
zh: 第一是时机。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the most important.
zh: 它是最重要的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the thing that you control the least.
zh: 它是你控制得最少的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's a lot of companies that have built amazing products, amazing services at the wrong time, in the right market and not won, right?
zh: 有很多公司在错误的时候建立了惊人的产品、惊人的服务，在正确的市场却没有赢，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so timing really matters.
zh: 所以时机真的很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't control timing, but if you don't have timing, you don't win.
zh: 你无法控制时机，但如果你没有时机，你就赢不了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Number two is the market.
zh: 第二是市场。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to be able to go after a large enough market, a chunk at a time.
zh: 你必须能够一个接一个地攻克足够大的市场。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you're not able to go out and prosecute a market a chunk at a time, but make sure that that keeps getting bigger and bigger, it's very hard to win.
zh: 如果你不能一个接一个地去开拓市场，但要确保那个市场越来越大，那么很难赢。

speaker: Unknown
en: So market tends to be the second most important thing in my mind after timing.
zh: 所以市场在我看来是仅次于时机的第二重要的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: The third one then is team.
zh: 第三是团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to have the right team.
zh: 你必须拥有合适的团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the team does not mean just people liking each other.
zh: 团队并不意味着仅仅是人们互相喜欢。

speaker: Unknown
en: Team means that it is actually well-rounded.
zh: 团队意味着它是真正全面均衡的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That means the things that you suck at someone else is really good at, and you have both accepted that of each other.
zh: 这意味着你不擅长的东西别人真的很擅长，并且你们彼此都接受了这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: For example, I have a person that I never go to another job without, and she is my partner in crime.
zh: 例如，我有一个人，我永远不会在没有他的情况下去另一份工作，她是我的死党。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the reason I have her is because she is so good at things that I'm not good at.
zh: 我之所以需要她，是因为她非常擅长我不擅长的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so she's able to, any job I've taken since I've been working with her, it's always, it's a combined deal.
zh: 所以她能够，自从我和她一起工作以来，我接受的每一份工作，总是，这是一个组合的交易。

speaker: Unknown
en: If we don't have two offers, we don't go.
zh: 如果我们要不到两个 offer，我们就不去。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so team is really important, a well-rounded team where people understand how to complement each other.
zh: 所以团队真的很重要，一个全面均衡的团队，人们知道如何互补。

speaker: Unknown
en: And by the way, in the team, sometimes people say, "Well, isn't team more important than market?"
zh: 顺便说一下，在团队中，有时人们会说，“嗯，团队不是比市场更重要吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: No, if you have a great market mediocre team, the market pulls you up.
zh: 不，如果你有一个伟大的市场和普通的团队，市场会把你拉上来。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you have a shitty market and a great team, the market drags you down.
zh: 如果你有一个糟糕的市场和一个伟大的团队，市场会把你拖下去。

speaker: Unknown
en: The market always wins.
zh: 市场总是赢家。

speaker: Unknown
en: So no, timing, market, team.
zh: 所以不，时机，市场，团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: Number four is product.
zh: 第四是产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think product is the soul of a company.
zh: 我认为产品是公司的灵魂。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the place where people seek value is, what are you delivering to me?
zh: 那是人们寻找价值的地方，你交付给我什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What problem are you solving to me gets manifested through the delivery of a product.
zh: 你为我解决什么问题通过产品的交付体现出来。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you have to make sure that you build a great product.
zh: 所以你必须确保你构建一个伟大的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: I actually think it's unethical to have a mediocre product sold in the market.
zh: 我实际上认为在市场上销售平庸的产品是不道德的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So timing, market team, product number five is brand.
zh: 所以时机，市场团队，产品第五是品牌。

speaker: Unknown
en: I had a mentor one time that told me, Mark Lewis, he said, "Jeetu, don't ever go to a company who's lost their brand mojo, because very hard to resurrect it back."
zh: 我曾经有一个导师告诉我，Mark Lewis，他说，“Jeetu，永远不要去一家失去了他们品牌魔力的公司，因为很难复活它。”

speaker: Unknown
en: If they have lost their product and you cannot fix the product.
zh: 如果他们失去了他们的产品，而你无法修复产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: But do you think Sybase is coming back?
zh: 但你认为 Sybase 会回来吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: No.
zh: 不。

speaker: Unknown
en: Once you lose your brand and once you lose the trust, people don't come back to you that much.
zh: 一旦你失去了你的品牌，一旦你失去了信任，人们就不会那么回到你身边了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's very hard to do.
zh: 这很难做到。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then number six is distribution.
zh: 然后第六是分销。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just because you build it, they will not come.
zh: 仅仅因为你构建了它，他们不会来。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to make sure that you figure out a scaled mechanism of getting that offering to many, many people.
zh: 你必须确保你找到一种大规模的机制，将该产品带给许多人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so timing trumps market, market trumps team.
zh: 所以时机胜过市场，市场胜过团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: Team trumps product, product trumps brand, brand trumps distribution.
zh: 团队胜过产品，产品胜过品牌，品牌胜过分销。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't have all six, you don't win.
zh: 如果你没有全部六个，你就赢不了。

speaker: Unknown
en: What an amazing nugget to have at the end here, just so I understand how you think about this is, do you have a template that you work through when you're thinking about a new business unit or new product to launch?
zh: 这里有这么一个精彩的小片段，只是为了让我理解你是怎么思考这个问题的，你在考虑一个新的业务单元或新产品要推出时，有一个可以套用的模板吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it is timing, right?
zh: 是时机，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is market, what market do we start with?
zh: 是市场，我们从哪个市场开始？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you actually operationalize?
zh: 你实际上是如何落地的？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's actually exactly like that.
zh: 实际上确实就像那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: I will ask myself the question on, "Is this the right time for us to go out and double, triple, quadruple down?"
zh: 我会问我自己这个问题，“现在是我们出去加倍投入、三倍投入、四倍投入的正确时机吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: We might still be in experimentation mode, but do I need to double down on this right now because this might not manifest for another seven years and then we're going to be too early.
zh: 我们可能还处于实验模式，但我需要现在就在这个上面加倍投入吗，因为这可能要七年之后才会显现，那我们就太早了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And by the way, you have to know the difference between a megatrend and a hype cycle.
zh: 顺便说一下，你必须知道「超级趋势」和「炒作周期」的区别。

speaker: Unknown
en: When there's a megatrend, don't fight it and don't succumb to the temptation of trying to go out and do vanity work for a hype cycle.
zh: 当有一个超级趋势时，不要对抗它，也不要屈服于诱惑，试图出去为炒作周期做虚荣的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's a big difference between the two.
zh: 这两者之间有很大的区别。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think having the judgment, the older you get, the better that judgment gets.
zh: 我认为拥有这种判断力，你越老，这种判断力就越好。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just miles.
zh: 真的是天差地别。

speaker: Unknown
en: But having that judgment is really important because you see a pattern recognition at some point.
zh: 但拥有这种判断力真的很重要，因为在某个时候你会看到模式识别。

speaker: Unknown
en: I imagine AI megatrend.
zh: 我猜 AI 是个超级趋势。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI is a megatrend in my mind, and there's a bunch of hype cycles we've had where I never particularly subscribe to them.
zh: 在我看来 AI 是一个超级趋势，而且我们有过一堆炒作周期，我从来没有特别认同过它们。

speaker: Unknown
en: The easiest way for me to tell is, the way it's described, is it easy to understand what this could do in its ultimate form for most people, or do you need to have a PhD to understand what someone's saying?
zh: 对我来说最简单的判断方法是，看它是怎么描述的，是不是很容易理解这东西最终形态能为大多数人做什么，还是你需要有个博士学位才能理解某人在说什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: When you feel like you need a PhD to understand what someone's saying, chances are it ain't going to be a megatrend, because by definition, a megatrend is it's going to impact a large population of the world.
zh: 当你觉得你需要有个博士学位才能理解某人在说什么时，它大概率不会是一个超级趋势，因为根据定义，超级趋势是它将影响世界上很大一部分人口。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if the thing is too complicated, chances are it's not going to have that level of effect, outsize effect.
zh: 如果这东西太复杂，大概率它不会有那种程度的影响，那种不成比例的影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's an awesome heuristic.
zh: 这是一个很棒的经验法则。

speaker: Unknown
en: I imagine you're thinking Web3 as a classic example.
zh: 我想你是在把 Web3 作为一个经典例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Web3 was the one that I actually cite all the time.
zh: Web3 是我实际上一直引用的那个例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: I couldn't understand what it had been.
zh: 我没法理解它是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And all of these people were kind like, "Oh, Web3, Web3."
zh: 所有的人都 kinda 像，“噢，Web3，Web3。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "I couldn't make a heads of tails out of a use case."
zh: 我就像，“我无法从使用场景中理出个头绪来。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But with AI, it's like you go to ChatGPT, you ask it a question, you get an answer.
zh: 但对于 AI，就像你去 ChatGPT，问它一个问题，你得到一个答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: I get this.
zh: 我懂这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is easy.
zh: 这很简单。

speaker: Unknown
en: So going back to your framework, just to kind of close the loop there, it's really interesting that timing is the first variable you look at.
zh: 所以回到你的框架，只是为了在那里闭环一下，非常有趣的是时机是你看的第一个变量。

speaker: Unknown
en: This could be an amazing idea.
zh: 这可能是一个绝妙的主意。

speaker: Unknown
en: You got the right team, amazing product that works really well, but the timing may just not be right.
zh: 你有了对的团队，运作良好的绝佳产品，但时机可能就是不对。

speaker: Unknown
en: And no matter how awesome it is, it's not going to work.
zh: 无论它有多棒，它是行不通的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Steve Jobs put away the iPad because he thought that the iPhone was a better idea.
zh: Steve Jobs 把 iPad 放到一边，因为他觉得 iPhone 是一个更好的主意。

speaker: Unknown
en: And timing wise, he actually made exactly the right call.
zh: 就时机而言，他实际上做出了完全正确的决定。

speaker: Unknown
en: The iPad became successful because of the iPhone success.
zh: iPad 之所以成功是因为 iPhone 的成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: The reverse order might have not had the same effect, but he had to make sure that he focused on one thing and he actually puts the other, he said, "The timing's not right, but I'm going to get back to it."
zh: 如果反过来顺序可能就不会有同样的效果，但他得确保他专注于一件事，他实际上把另一个搁置了，他说，“时机不对，但我会回到它的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So by the way, when timing is wrong doesn't mean that you scrapped the idea.
zh: 所以顺便说一下，当时机不对并不意味着你废弃了这个想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: It just means that you might put it on ice for a bit.
zh: 它只是意味着你可能把它暂时搁置一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a lot of that happening right now where people try to do a thing and now AI actually makes it possible.
zh: 现在有很多这样的事情发生，人们试图做一件事，现在 AI 实际上使其成为可能。

speaker: Unknown
en: And now they're like, "Oh shit, it was way too early."
zh: 现在他们就像，“噢该，这太早了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And the other thing you have to keep in mind is you have to also be good enough to know that when something is going to be ready in six months, you can't think about what it's doing today.
zh: 你必须记住的另一件事是，你也必须足够好地知道当某样东西六个月后准备好时，你不能去想它今天在做什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI is moving so fast right now.
zh: AI 现在发展得太快了。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the things I tell my team is, fast-forward six months from now and anticipate what that's going to do and get prepared for that world.
zh: 我告诉我团队的一件事是，从现在起快进六个月，预判那会做什么，并为那个世界做好准备。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't get prepared for the world of today thinking that you're not going to be able to get there because in six months your assumption sets are going to be different.
zh: 不要为了今天的世界做准备，想着你到不了那里，因为六个月后你的假设集合将会不同。

speaker: Unknown
en: And please don't actually then bias yourself with the assumption sets you have right now to not move forward.
zh: 并且请不要实际上然后用你现在的假设集合来偏袒自己，从而不前进。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the worst things I think companies do sometimes is they put too much emphasis only on solely on experience.
zh: 我认为公司有时做的最糟糕的事情之一是，他们只过分强调仅仅依靠经验。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think experience is good, but experience can actually be meaningfully bad in some areas where you get too biased.
zh: 我认为经验是好的，但经验实际上在某些领域可能是非常有坏处的，在那里你会变得太有偏见。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so you almost have to say that I have to have the ability to unlearn.
zh: 所以你几乎必须说我必须有去学习的能力。

speaker: Unknown
en: And combination of experience with complete inexperience is what creates the magic because the inexperience allows you to ask questions that you might have never had with experience.
zh: 经验与完全没有经验的结合创造了魔法，因为没有经验允许你提出你可能从未有过经验的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the combo of those two gives you the best of the pattern recognition, plus the charting new territory that's never been walked on before.
zh: 这两者的结合给你最好的模式识别，加上绘制以前从未涉足的新领域。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, this is a trend I've been hearing on this podcast that people worry about young people and people graduating out of college right now and jobs and AI.
zh: 是的，这是我在这个播客上听到的一种趋势，人们担心年轻人和刚大学毕业的人以及工作和 AI。

speaker: Unknown
en: But they're the people that are most open-minded about what AI can do for them and how to harness AI and not code in the way people have always coded.
zh: 但他们是对 AI 能为他们做什么以及如何利用 AI 以及不用人们一直以来的编码方式最持开放态度的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just like, okay, this is the way it works now.
zh: 就像这样，好吧，这就是现在的工作方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Experience, Lenny, can jade us, and I always say when people say, "Oh, entry-level people will never be hired again."
zh: 经验，Lenny，会让我们变得愤世嫉俗，我总是说当人们说，“哦，初级人员再也不会被录用了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the stupidest thing a company can do, because now what you've done is you have completely shut the door to new fresh ideas.
zh: 这是公司能做的最愚蠢的事情，因为现在你所做的就是你已经完全关闭了新思想的大门。

speaker: Unknown
en: I cannot think today the way I thought when I was 19.
zh: 今天我无法像我 19 岁时那样思考。

speaker: Unknown
en: There is just no way that I can do that.
zh: 我根本无法那样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: But what I can try to do is I can try to make sure I surround myself with enough amount of my time to get exposed to that thinking and then couple it with what I know and maybe have something better than what either of those two could have had by themselves.
zh: 但我可以试着做的是，我可以试着确保我投入足够的时间让自己接触那种思维，然后将其与我所知道的东西结合起来，也许能产生比这两者单独存在时更好的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, with that, Jeetu, we have reached our very exciting lightning round.
zh: 好了，Jeetu，我们已经到了我们非常激动人心的闪电战环节。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've got five questions for you.
zh: 我有五个问题要问你。

speaker: Unknown
en: Are you ready?
zh: 你准备好了吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: All right.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: First question, what are two or three books that you find yourself recommending most to other people?
zh: 第一个问题，你发现自己最常推荐给别人的两三本书是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: The Bible and Tech in My Mind, there's Innovator's Dilemma and Innovator's Solution from Clayton Christensen.
zh: 《圣经》和《我脑海中的科技》，有 Clayton Christensen 的《创新者的窘境》和《创新者的解决方案》。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think you have to read that book and I'd say I'd recommend to people that read it every few years.
zh: 我认为你必须读那本书，我会建议人们每隔几年读一遍。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the other one that I love is Ben Horowitz's book.
zh: 另一本我喜欢的是 Ben Horowitz 的书。

speaker: Unknown
en: Hard Thing about Hard Things really talks about how you manage your psychology when things get hard.
zh: 《关于难事最难的事》真的讲到了当事情变得艰难时你如何管理你的心理。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think those are the ones, I am not a big believer that you keep reading thousands of books all the time because I think to me, retention really matters.
zh: 我想就是这些，我不是一个一直读几千本书的坚定信徒，因为对我来说，保留真的很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: And my brain's just not that big that I can retain that much.
zh: 我的大脑没那么大，记不住那么多东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I tend to distill the essence of a few things quite a bit more, and at least as the older I've gotten, I've actually used that pattern more.
zh: 所以，我倾向于更多地提炼几件事的精髓，至少随着我年纪变大，我实际上更多地使用了这种模式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Favorite recent movie or TV show that you've really enjoyed?
zh: 你最近非常喜欢的一部最喜欢的电影或电视节目？

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't remember the name of it, but the Brad Pitt F1 movie that I saw that was pretty cool.
zh: 我不记得它的名字了，但我看过的 Brad Pitt 的 F1 电影挺酷的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Wait, it was a recent Brad Pitt movie?
zh: 等等，那是最近的 Brad Pitt 的电影吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Lenny
en: Was it F1?
zh: 是 F1 吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: It was F1.
zh: 是 F1。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it was called F1, but it was pretty cool.
zh: 我想它叫 F1，但它挺酷的。

speaker: Unknown
en: For Best Fix-
zh: 对于最佳补救——

speaker: Unknown
en: Zach Brown is a good friend of mine and we were big supporters of McLaren, and so it was actually pretty cool to watch that movie.
zh: Zach Brown 是我的好朋友，我们是 McLaren 的坚定支持者，所以看那部电影其实挺酷的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh man, I bet.
zh: 噢伙计，我敢打赌。

speaker: Unknown
en: So many stories I haven't tapped into.
zh: 太多我还没有挖掘的故事了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Favorite product you recently discovered that you really love?
zh: 你最近发现的你真正喜欢的最喜欢的产品？

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, it's cliche, but I feel like what ChatGPT, Gemini and Claude have done, it's changed lives.
zh: 我的意思是，这很老套，但我觉得 ChatGPT、Gemini 和 Claude 所做的，它改变了生活。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's changed my life in the way that I learn in some ridiculous ways.
zh: 它以某些可笑的方式改变了我学习的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I actually feel like when I got this new job to run all product for Cisco, there's zero chance I would've been able to do it if AI wasn't there.
zh: 所以我实际上觉得，当我得到这份新工作去负责 Cisco 的所有产品时，如果 AI 不存在，零机会我能做到。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because I didn't know anything about so many domains that we were in.
zh: 因为我对我们要进入的许多领域一无所知。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I had to get an accelerated training course within a matter of three months.
zh: 我不得不在三个月的时间内接受加速培训课程。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I mean, I worked around the clock during that time, but I could have worked around the clock without the tooling and I would've been nowhere near as effective.
zh: 我的意思是，在那段时间我日夜不停地工作，但如果没有这些工具，我本可以日夜不停地工作，但效果远不及现在。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I feel like those three have done an amazing thing.
zh: 所以我觉得这三者做了很棒的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And Grok, even what you're seeing with Grok tied to Twitter is pretty amazing.
zh: 还有 Grok，甚至你在 Grok 与 Twitter 挂钩方面看到的也相当惊人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Wow, that's a profound statement.
zh: 哇，这是一个深刻的声明。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've never heard that before from someone at your level that you feel like you wouldn't be able to have done this job without AI.
zh: 我以前从未从你在这个级别的人那里听过这个，你觉得如果没有 AI 你就无法做这份工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's your chance.
zh: 这是你的机会。

speaker: Unknown
en: Especially for someone without the background in networking and hardware, that is so interesting.
zh: 特别是对于没有网络和硬件背景的人来说，这太有趣了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's amazing how just at every level, AI is helping at the most bottom end and also in your level.
zh: AI 在每个层面都提供帮助，在最底层以及在你这个层面，这真是令人惊叹。

speaker: Unknown
en: Most people don't realize, I fundamentally believe this is the reason that I'm able to enjoy some of the experiences I have.
zh: 大多数人没有意识到，我根本相信这就是我能够享受我拥有的一些体验的原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was lucky enough that I had made enough money before this job.
zh: 我很幸运我在这份工作之前赚了足够的钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: That was not the thing that was actually holding us back, but the reason I'm able to experience some of the things that this job afforded me to experience have not even been remotely possible without AI, no chance that would have happened.
zh: 那实际上并不是阻碍我们的东西，但我能够体验这份工作让我体验到的一些事情的原因，如果没有 AI 是绝对不可能的，不可能发生的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Unreal.
zh: 难以置信。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, two more questions.
zh: 好的，还有两个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 嗯。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you have a favorite life motto that you often come back to and work your own life?
zh: 你有没有自己经常回归并用于经营人生的座右铭？

speaker: Unknown
en: You already shared a couple, but is there anything else or you want to double down on one you've already shared?
zh: 你已经分享了几个，但还有其他的吗，还是想重申一下你已经分享过的？

speaker: Unknown
en: Stamina trumps intellect.
zh: 毅力胜过才智。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like it's very important to have smart people, but you can become smart if you have curiosity and hunger and staying power and persistence.
zh: 我觉得拥有聪明人很重要，但如果你有好奇心、渴望、耐力和毅力，你就能变得聪明。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think that trait of learning to learn and constantly being hungry and having the stamina and persistence is far more important than the absolute measure of intellect that you might have, because that is very, very trainable and learnable over time and improvable over time.
zh: 所以我认为这种“学会学习”的特质，保持持续的渴望，拥有毅力和坚持，远比你可能拥有的绝对智力水平更重要，因为后者是非常、非常可训练的，随着时间推移是可学习的，也是可提升的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But hunger is very, it's not teachable is what I've found.
zh: 但这种渴望，根据我的发现，它是不可教授的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I 100% agree with that.
zh: 我百分之百同意这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Interestingly, when you watch AI work, it's just like, partly the reason it's so good is it just keeps trying.
zh: 有趣的是，当你观察 AI 工作时，就像，部分原因是它之所以这么好，就是因为它在不断尝试。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just like, "Okay, this didn't work.
zh: 就像是，“好吧，这行不通。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let keep going.
zh: 继续试。

speaker: Unknown
en: What else can we just keep trying?
zh: 还有什么我们可以继续尝试的？

speaker: Unknown
en: Just give me half an hour, I'll figure this out.
zh: 就给我半小时，我会搞清楚的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, last question.
zh: 好的，最后一个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: So when you were younger, you worked at Sizzler Steakhouse making $4 an hour is what I read.
zh: 所以当你年轻的时候，你在 Sizzler 牛排馆工作，据我所读，时薪 4 美元。

speaker: Unknown
en: 2.25, not four.

speaker: Unknown
en: It was below minimum wage 2.25, but we got tips.
zh: 那是低于最低工资的 2.25，但我们有小费。

speaker: Unknown
en: We got tips though, so that was-
zh: 不过我们确实收到了小费，所以那是——

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I see.
zh: 我明白了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Did you have a favorite dish at Sizzler, is my question?
zh: 你在 Sizzler 有没有最喜欢的菜肴，这是我的问题？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 有的。

speaker: Unknown
en: They used to have this Malibu chicken dish was like magic.
zh: 他们以前有一道 Malibu Chicken 鸡肉料理简直像魔法一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then it was probably, I don't know if people know this, and I know this is rapid fire, but I used to stutter when I started working at Sizzler and Sizzler is what allowed me to break out of my shell and not stutter.
zh: 然后可能是，我不知道大家是否知道这个，我知道这是快速问答，但我开始在 Sizzler 工作时口吃，而 Sizzler 让我走出了自己的外壳，不再口吃。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because something changed in my brain, but I'm like, I have to entertain people and if I don't, then they're not going to give me a good tip.
zh: 因为我大脑里有些东西改变了，我想，我必须娱乐大家，如果我不这样做，他们就不会给我好的小费。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the stuttering went away at Sizzler, and so I have an immense debt of gratitude and I think everyone should work in hospitality for a while in their younger years.
zh: 所以口吃在 Sizzler 消失了，所以我对此怀有巨大的感激之情，我认为每个人都应该在年轻的时候从事一段时间的服务业。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm kind of sad that my daughter has no interest in doing that because I'm like, "I wish she just worked as a waitress somewhere for a bit."
zh: 我有点难过，我女儿对做这件事毫无兴趣，因为我会想，“我希望她就在某个地方做一阵子女服务员。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's just so important to just, there's so many lessons on, I cleaned toilets at the restaurant.
zh: 这一点非常重要，有太多的教训，我在餐厅打扫过厕所。

speaker: Unknown
en: I actually washed dishes.
zh: 我确实洗过盘子。

speaker: Unknown
en: I actually waited on tables.
zh: 我确实服务过餐桌。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it was the best experience I had.
zh: 那是我有过最好的经历。

speaker: Unknown
en: It shaped me for what was to come in the most profound way.
zh: 它以最深远的方式塑造了未来的我。

speaker: Unknown
en: Jeetu, you're just endlessly full of wisdom.
zh: Jeetu，你真是充满了无穷的智慧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Two final questions.
zh: 最后两个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Where can folks find you?
zh: 大家在哪里可以找到你？

speaker: Unknown
en: Where do you want to point people to learn more about you, what you're up to, and how can listeners be useful to you?
zh: 你希望大家去哪里了解更多关于你、你的近况，以及听众如何能对你有所帮助？

speaker: Unknown
en: Where you can find me is ... I tend to ... A lot of people will ask the more success you encounter or the more people want to get mentored by you and learn from the experiences you've had, and I have run out of cycles to be able to do that on a one-on-one basis.
zh: 你在哪里可以找到我是……我倾向于……很多人会问，随着你获得越多的成功，或者越多人想要寻求你的指导并从你的经验中学习，而我已经没有精力去一对一地做这些事了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what I try to do is do a lot of that on LinkedIn and Twitter, but largely I do a lot of that on LinkedIn.
zh: 所以我试图做的是在 LinkedIn 和 Twitter 上做很多这类事，但主要是在 LinkedIn 上。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so find me on LinkedIn.
zh: 所以在 LinkedIn 上找我。

speaker: Unknown
en: I tend to be very open about not just the work stuff, but the non-work stuff to do that.
zh: 我倾向于不仅对工作相关的事情非常坦诚，对非工作的事情也是如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: How can people be useful to me?
zh: 人们怎么能对我有用？

speaker: Unknown
en: Was that the question?
zh: 那是那个问题吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: What was the last question?
zh: 上一个问题是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: That is, how can listeners be useful to you?
zh: 那是，听众如何能对你有所帮助？

speaker: Unknown
en: How can listeners be useful to me?
zh: 听众如何能对我有帮助？

speaker: Unknown
en: If there is ... I would say that if you got something out of this session and if you get something out of whatever you learn from social media, just pay it forward and help the next person out a little bit more.
zh: 如果有……我想说的是，如果你从这次环节中获得了一些东西，如果你从你在社交媒体上学到的东西中获得了一些东西，那就传递下去，多帮助下一个人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yesterday I was at a talk and someone pulled me aside and said, "Hey, I saw your LinkedIn post about this."
zh: 昨天我在一个讲座上，有人把我拉到一边说，“嘿，我看到了你关于这个的 LinkedIn 帖子。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't be stingy with words, and Jeetu, since then, I've been going to see my parents once every two months or so in India, and when I see them, I tell them that I love them all the time.
zh: 不要吝啬语言，Jeetu，从那以后，我每两个月左右回一次印度看望我的父母，当我见到他们时，我一直告诉他们我爱他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: Literally, what could be more rewarding to me than that?
zh: 字面上说，对我来说还有什么比这更有回报的呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: It was amazing that they were able to go out and have joy brought to their lives as a result of something they got inspired by something that I learned in my life, that's paid forward.
zh: 他们能够走出去，因受到我人生中学到的东西的启发而给他们的生活带来快乐，这就是薪火相传，这真是太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm excited to hear the stories that come out of this conversation.
zh: 我很期待听到这次对话产生的那些故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Jeetu, thank you so much for being here.
zh: Jeetu，非常感谢你来到这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you for having me.
zh: 谢谢你的邀请。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was great.
zh: 很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: Bye everyone.
zh: 大家再见。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you so much for listening.
zh: 非常感谢大家的收听。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you found this valuable, you can subscribe to the show on Apple Podcasts, Spotify, or your favorite podcast app.
zh: 如果你觉得这有价值，你可以在 Apple Podcasts、Spotify 或你最喜欢的播客应用上订阅这个节目。

speaker: Unknown
en: Also, please consider giving us a rating or leaving a review as that really helps other listeners find the podcast.
zh: 另外，请考虑给我们打分或留下评论，因为这真的能帮助其他听众找到这个播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can find all past episodes or learn more about the show at lennyspodcast.com.
zh: 你可以在 lennyspodcast.com 找到所有往期节目或了解更多关于这个节目的信息。

speaker: Unknown
en: See you in the next episode.
zh: 下一集见。
